entry.title: Multiple Address Handling
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

We are using SAP Cloud ERP and have business partners that operate multiple factories and plants. We are currently debating whether to register them as a single BP using the "Multiple Address Handling" feature or to create a separate BP for each plant.
Since these partners act as both customers and suppliers, I would like to know if Multiple Address Handling is still unavailable for the supplier (vendor) function?

Best Regards,
Saki

entry.title: Joule Studio Enablement Guide
entry.content:
📑 Table of Contents

  1. Overview
  2. Use Case & Mission Description
  3. Prerequisites
  4. Setting Up SAP Build for Joule Studio
  5. Required Subscriptions & Instances in BTP
  6. Mandatory Roles & Role Collections
  7. Ensuring Required Subscriptions Are Available
  8. Adding Missing Cloud Identity Service (Additional Tenant)
  9. Formation Creation (Mandatory for Joule Integration)
  10. Enablement Complete

 

1. Overview

Joule Studio is SAP’s platform to build Joule Agents, Joule Skills, and integrate enterprise AI capabilities directly into SAP applications. It is part of SAP Build, enabling teams to create and extend business applications and AI solutions with built-in governance and clean core alignment.

 

2. Use Case & Mission Description

  • Joule Studio is activated
  • Required BTP services are set up
  • Identity and trust between Joule and IAS are configured
  • Administrators can deploy Joule agents
  • Developers can create Joule Skills, Document Grounding, and integrate custom APIs

3. Prerequisites

To activate Joule Studio, the following must be fulfilled:

Required Entitlements

  • SAP Build Process Automation — Subscriptions(build_default)
  • Joule — Subscriptions(standard)
  • SAP Cloud Identity Services — Subscriptions(additional-tenant)

Cloud Identity Services (Mandatory)

IAS is required for:

  • Authentication
  • SSO
  • Authorization management
  • Trust configuration for Joule

If you do not have IAS, you must create a new tenant.

 

4. Setting Up SAP Build for Joule Studio

Standalone activation of:

  • Joule
  • SAP Build Process Automation

 

SAP BTP Boosters/Subscriptions

Boosters automate service activation.
Navigate:

Global Account → Boosters or Create Subscriptions of each of the plans given.

Use the following:

  • “Set Up SAP Build Process Automation”
  • “Set Up Joule”

Each booster/Subscriptions:

  • Assigns roles
  • Creates role collections
  • Subscribes necessary services

 

5. Required Subscriptions & Instances in BTP

Mandatory

Service

Plan

Required For

SAP Build Process Automation

build_default

Joule Studio backend

Joule

standard

Joule Studio AI

SAP Cloud Identity Services

Additional-Tenant

Authentication, SSO

 

6. Mandatory Roles & Role Collections

The Joule Booster does NOT create a role collection.

You MUST manually create one:

Create Role Collection

  1. Subaccount → Security → Role Collections
  2. Click Create
  3. Name it: Joule_Role_Collection

Add Roles

Navigate to:
Security → Role Collections → Joule_Role_Collection → Edit

Add:

  • extensibility_developer
  • end_user

Save.

Assign to Your User

Security → Users
Assign: Joule_Role_Collection

 

7. Ensure All Required Subscriptions Are Visible in the Systems

Under:
Global Account → System Landscape → Systems

Confirm:

  • Joule (standard)
  • SAP Build Process Automation (build_default)
  • SAP Cloud Identity Services (Additional Tenant)*

(*) If missing, add manually (next section).

 

8. Adding Cloud Identity Service (Additional Tenant)

If IAS additional tenant is not visible:

Navigate:

Global Account → System Landscape → Systems → Service Owner View

Click Add System

Enter:

  • System Type: SAP Cloud Identity Services
  • System Name: any name
  • CLD Tenant ID: IAS tenant ID

Finding CLD Tenant ID

Use Cloud Reporting:

🔹 Live Landscape
https://reporting.ondemand.com/sap/crp/cdo?query=xxx&type=crp_search

🔹 Canary Landscape
https://spc-vlab.ondemand.com/sap/crp/cdo?query=xxx&type=crp_search

Replace xxx with your IAS tenant ID.

⏳Note: It may take up to 24 hours for reporting to sync newly created IAS tenants.

 

9. Formation Creation (Mandatory for Joule Integration)

Navigate:
Subaccount → System Landscape → Formations → Create Formation

1. General Information

  • Formation Name: Your Choice
  • Formation Type: Integration with Joule

2. Include Systems

Add:

  • SAP Build Process Automation
  • Joule
  • SAP Cloud Identity Services

3. Integration Details

Check the box:
Enable Capability Deployment

4. Create

System may take time (~10–30 minutes) to reach Ready status.

This deployment links Joule, BPA, and IAS together.

 

 

10. Enablement Complete — You Can Build Joule Agents

Once the Formation is Ready:

You can now:

  • Build Joule Agents
  • Build Joule Skills
  • Use Document Grounding
  • Call Custom APIs

entry.title: Entitlements
entry.content:
Introduction : 

In the SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP), Entitlements play a crucial role in defining how services and resources are allocated across different environments. They act as the foundation for enabling platform capabilities, ensuring that organizations can control usage based on purchased or subscribed service plans.

Understanding Entitlements becomes even more important when managing large landscapes that include multiple Global Accounts, Directories, and Subaccounts. This is where Entity Assignments and Service Assignments come into the picture. Entity Assignments allow administrators to distribute entitled service plans to specific subaccounts or directories, while Service Assignments focus on enabling and configuring those services within a given subaccount.

In this blog, we will explore the concept of Entitlements in SAP BTP and take a closer look at how Entity Assignments and Service Assignments work together to ensure proper service availability, governance, and resource management across the platform.




1 What is  Entitlements ?          

Entitlements in SAP BTP are the authorized usage of specific SAP BTP services and service plans within a global account

2 Types of Entitlements in SAP BTP ?

1 Entity Assignment

2 Service Assignment

 1 Entity Assignment ?

Entity Assignment is the process of assigning entitlements from the Global Account to a Subaccount.

Why Entity Assignment is Needed?

Because:

  • Entitlements are owned by Global Account
  • Applications are run inside Subaccounts
  • So you must assign first, then use.

 2 Service Assignment?

Service Assignment is assigning a specific service plan to a Subaccount or Space so developers can create instances.

How to Navigate to Entity Assignment

  • First we need to login BTP Cockpit
  • After click on Go To Your Trial Account shown in below.
    • After we can see Entitlements here

      => After we need to click on Entity Assignments

           Here we need to mention our Subaccounts/Directories:

      And here we can see services

      => After we will click on Edit Button
      we can see Add Service Plans Button here

      • Here Click on Add Service Plans Button

      After we can see Add service Plans  here select one service and click on Add service Plans Button

      • After in search bar search which service you need to add

                 This is all about Entity Assignment

        Service Assignment:

        When you click on Service Assignment we can see all available service here


        Conclusion : 

        Entitlements form the backbone of service management in SAP BTP by controlling which services and plans are available within an organization’s landscape. Through Entity Assignments, administrators can strategically distribute these entitlements to directories and subaccounts based on project, team, or business requirements. Service Assignments then take this a step further by enabling the actual consumption and configuration of services within specific subaccounts.

        By clearly understanding the relationship between Entitlements, Entity Assignments, and Service Assignments, organizations can ensure optimal resource utilization, maintain governance, and avoid unnecessary service limitations. Proper entitlement management not only improves operational efficiency but also supports scalable and secure cloud adoption within SAP BTP environments.

entry.title: Principal propagation setup as SAP Java backend and SAP Business Technology Platform
entry.content:
Principal Propagation Setup as Java Backend

SAP BTP =>Cloud Connector =>SAP NetWeaver AS Java

  1. Introduction

This blog explains how to configure principal propagation using X.509 certificate for an SAP NetWeaver AS Java backend (HTTPS). SAP Cloud Connector generates a client certificate for the user and forward it to the backend system.

  1. Architecture Overview

User =>BTP App/Work Zone =>BTP Destination (PrincipalPropagation) => SCC => AS Java Backend.

Key requirements:

  • Mutual TLS between SCC and backend for HTTPS propagation (backend must trust SCC system cert/CA chain).
  • CA Certificate configured in SCC can issue user certificate.
  • User mapping rules on AS Java (ClientCertLoginModule) to map certificate fields to UME users.
  1. Prerequisites Checklist

3.1 Backend user attribute alignment (SU01)

SAP Backend user email/user ID must match with BTP identity.

Ensure the email in the backend user record matches the email used to log in to BTP.

3.2 Cloud Connector certificates

You need three certificate

  1. System Certificate (for trust between SCC and backend)
  2. CA Certificate (to issue client certificate)
  3. User Sample Certificate (rule based mappting).

NOTE : SAP Supports Self-signed certificate for ABAP based systems, but Java not supports Self-signed and the SCC system certificate/Backend server certificate must be signed by CA.

Java Systems: Java-based systems (like SAP Portal or PI/PO) use a KeyStore-based trust model. While they can technically support self-signed certificates, But SAP's implementation for Principal Propagation via the Cloud Connector requires a Certificate Authority (CA) chain for validation.

3.3 Permissions & access

-SCC admin access

-BTP subaccount admin for Destinations

-AS Java admin access to NetWeaver Administrator (NWA) security and SSL configuration.

  1. Step-by-Step Configuration

Step 1 - Maintain backend user mapping attribute (Email)

In backend user maintenance (ABAP SU01 / Java UME as applicable), fill E-Mail Address exactly as used in BTP.

Pick one mapping strategy (Email OR UserID) and use it consistently across BTP <-> IdP <->backend.

Here we taken Email strategy.

Step 2 - Generate/Import SCC certificates (System + CA + Sample User Cert)

2.1 System Certificate (SCC)

In Cloud Connector Admin UI:

  1. Go to Configuration -> On Premise -> System Certificate
  2. Create CSR and get it signed by CA (recommended).

  1. Import the certificate received from your AD team.

 

Also import the signed system certificate in AS Java trust store (NWA=>Cert & keys=>key storage=>Trusted CA).

NOTE: Import chain certificate (binded=>actual certificate and RootCA).

2.2 CA Certificate (SCC)

  1. Go to Configuration -> On Premise -> CA Certificate
  2. CA Certificate can be self-signed or the same way you can generate CSR & import the signed CA certificate.

 

2.3 User Sample Certificate

Maintain subject pattern as email and generate the sample certificate.

 

Create sample certificate.

 

-Import the created sample certificate in NWA=>Cert & keys=>key storage=>TicketKetstore

Step 3 - Sync Cloud Trust with IdP in SCC

Sync the trust configuration for principal propagation.

  1. Go to Cloud To On-Premise -> Principal Propagation
  2. Click Sync to fetch IdP/trust configuration from the subaccount context.

 

Without this trust, SCC can’t validate the inbound identity and won’t be able to generate the user certificate.

Step 4 - Configure cloud to On-prem mapping Your backend system mapping must be configured for HTTPS and identity forwarding.

  1. Go to Cloud To On-Premise → Access Control
  2. Create or edit your mapping for the backend system (HTTPS)
  3. Set Principal Type => X.509 Certificate
  4. Maintain resource paths (for example / or /sap/ depending on your requirement).

 

Set Principal Type = X.509 Certificate for the certificate-based identity.

 

Step 5 - Configure BTP Destination Authentication = PrincipalPropagation

In BTP:

  1. Navigate to Connectivity -> Destinations
  2. Create/update the Runtime destination to the backend via SCC
  3. Set:

Proxy Type: OnPremise

Type : HTTP

Authentication: PrincipalPropagation

URL points to virtual host/port from SCC mapping

  1. Backend Setup (SAP NetWeaver AS Java)

Step 6 - Activate HTTPS port in AS Java

AS Java must accept HTTPS and be able to request/require client certificates.

Configure SSL:

  1. Open: http://<host>:<port>/nwa     =>ssl
  2. Add a new access point with protocol HTTPS (ignore if HTTPS already active)

  1. Ensure SSL library is available and the SSL status becomes OK
  1. Secure/Apply SSL certificate for Java backend.

Your Java HTTPS endpoint certificate must be valid for the hostname used by SCC.

  1. Create/generate CSR for CA signing and import your CA response and root CA

     

Note : While generate the CSR don’t, forgot to maintain SAN (Subject Alternate Name) address, without SAN URL cannot be secured.

Tip: SAN can be FQDN and hostname of Java backend.(if you have two java instance generate CN = *.domain.com.

You can refer the SAP Note to generate the CSR & Certificate import using sapgenpse cmd line.

       2275562 - How to import sapgenpse generated PSE file into AS Java

Step 7 - Define Rule-Based Mapping (ClientCertLoginModule)

Converting the certificate identity into a UME user.

-Add ClientCertLoginModule

-Define rules for mapping certificate fields (Subject CN / SubjectAlternativeName/email) to a UME user.

NWA=>Authentication and Single Sign-On =>Authentication

Maintain below authentication stacks for Policy client_cert.

 

- Configure Clientcertloginmodule (Create Mapping rules)

-Enable Certificate Logon

-PrincipalMappingLoginModule

Step 8 – Define Reverse Proxy parameters

Add the parameter
icm/trusted_reverse_proxy_<X> = <SCC System Certificate Subject, Issuer>

This reverse proxy parameter used to establish the trust relationship between the SAP Backend and SAP Cloud connector.

NOTE : This is mandatory for Principal propagation.

Step 9 – Restart the Java services

  • Verify everything and restart the java services to make the parameters effect.

Step 10 – Validate the SSO functionality with the work zone application URL.

SUMMARY:
After completing the successful principal propagation setup as java backend, below are the recommendations.

  1. For Java based systems use the CA signed certificate.
  2. Ensure SAN entries are added in the SSL certificate.
  3. Generate sample certificate in SCC and import it in Ticketkeystore of NWA.
  4. Maintain only required fields => AttributeName, getUserFrom and UserMappingMode for ClientCertLoginModule.

 Thank you.

entry.title: Re: how can create an app variante using adapt UI and ass...
entry.content:
Good day @Hichem_Rahmani

Yes, your understanding is correct.

To reiterate, using spaces/pages only controls whether the app variant tile is shown on the user’s homepage. It does not automatically provide a hard authorisation control, so a user with the relevant app authorisation may still be able to find or start the app variant from the search bar.

If you need stricter control, you need to check whether your system supports IAM App Authorisation Variants for the app and then assign that only to your intended business roles.

See the reference below for more details on how to work with IAM Apps

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/53e36b5493804bcdb3f6f14de8b487dd/e2b39fb01689420393931a9eb3f627ec.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/53e36b5493804bcdb3f6f14de8b487dd/b9fd81647cdf45f5ad462a50222c2caa.html?locale=en-US

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: How BC Sets Work with Transport Requests in SAP
entry.content:
Business Configuration Sets (BC Sets) in SAP are tools used to capture and reuse Customizing (SPRO) configuration settings. They allow you to bundle configuration entries into a reusable object and move them across systems (DEV → QA → PRD) in a controlled way. 

BC Sets & Transport Request – How They Work Together 

  • When you create or record a BC Set (using T-code SCPR3), the system assigns it to a Transport Request. 
  • The BC Set definition (not the actual table entries) is saved in the transport. 
  • Once the Transport Request (SE10) is released, it can be imported into the target system. 
  • In the target system, the BC Set must be activated (SCPR20) to apply the configuration data to the tables.

 

When used? 

  • Template-based rollout projects 
  • S/4HANA migrations 
  • Multi-country implementations 
  • Reusable configuration packages

Taking example of this TR:

We'll create through TCode SCPR3

We'll give name following Z* and select type of BC set form (Transport Request).

After Activation and all we'll select particular BC Set and click on Download

After successful download we'll get below message. 

Now we'll select the Target System and Upload this file. (Transaction Code: SCPR3)

Here we'll click on upload option and select the correct downloaded file with ( .bcs ) extension.

Now we’ll activate it. (Transaction Code: SCPR20)

Conclusion: BC Sets are a structured way to capture, transport, and reuse SAP Customizing settings. While the Transport Request moves the BC Set object between systems, the actual configuration is applied only after activation in the target system. 

In short, BC Sets provide better control, reusability, and standardization of configuration compared to normal direct transports — especially useful in rollout and S/4HANA projects.

entry.title: Re: Displaying a Hidden Field from the Compensation Works...
entry.content:
Hi @Joe0603 

Since 2H 2025 you can make a field visible only on the worksheet, only visible on the profile, hidden on both or visible on both. But you can't have it hidden on the worksheet for a specific group of planners but visible on the profile for that same group.

Here are the tags you are looking for : hideInProfile="false" hideInForm="false"

 

Hope this helps.

Best,

Xavier

entry.title: Step-by-Step Guide to Create a Transparent Table in SE11 (ABAP Dictionary)
entry.content:
In ABAP development, database tables form the backbone of application data storage. Among the different table types, transparent tables are the most commonly used because they directly map to database tables and store business data efficiently.

Understanding how to create a transparent table is a fundamental skill for every ABAP developer, especially for beginners who are starting with the ABAP Dictionary. In this blog, we will walk through a step-by-step approach to creating a simple transparent table using transaction SE11, along with an explanation of key concepts such as delivery class, data elements, domains, and technical settings.

By the end of this guide, you will have a clear understanding of the complete process and be able to create your own tables confidently.

In ABAP development, database tables are created using the ABAP Dictionary (SE11).
Transparent tables are used to store application data in the database.

1. Go to T Code SE11.

2. Enter database name as shown below.

Name should be start with Z or Y. In our case table name is ZDB_BANK.

3. Click on create.

4. Enter Short Description (It is mandatory).

5. Select delivery class (It is also mandatory).

Delivery Class: It ensures how data will transport between different SAP system landscapes. There are seven types of delivery class but mostly we use Application & Customizing.

6. Select Data browser/Table View Editing (It is mandatory).

Data browser/Table View Editing: It specifies whether maintenance is allowed or not.

7. Enter fields of the table and their data elements. We can also use built-in data type.

Built-in Type: They are standard data-type provided by SAP. Customization is very limited, like we cannot provide the field label for the column. Also, we cannot use domain in this case.

Data Element: It specifies the semantic characteristics of the field.

Steps to create Data Element:

  • Enter the desired data element name (starting with Z or Y) in the column – data element.
  • Double clicks on the name and click on create.
  • Enter the short description & field label.

 

  • Enter the domain name or built-in type.

Domain: It specifies the technical characteristics of the field like value range or data-type etc.

  • Enter the domain name and double clicks on it.
  • Enter short description and data-type.

  • Click on activate

  • Press esc button.
  • Activate data element.
  • Press esc and you will redirect to the table.
  • Once done with all the fields, click on Technical Setting.

Technical Setting: It specifies the technical aspects of the table like data class, size, function group etc.

Data Class: It specifies which type of data this table will store. For example, APP0 for master data and APP1 for transaction data.

Size Category: It is the size of the lot assigned for the table in the memory at a time.
It is used for reservation of initial memory space in the database.

  • Create and save.

Steps to create entries:

  • Click on utilities.

  • Click on create entries under Table Contents

Steps to display entries:

  • Click on table icon

  • Entries will display.

Conclusion:

In this blog, we explored the end-to-end process of creating a transparent table in the ABAP Dictionary using SE11. We covered not only the technical steps but also the purpose of important components like data elements, domains, delivery class, and technical settings.

Creating database tables is one of the foundational tasks in ABAP development, and mastering these basics helps build a strong understanding of how SAP applications store and manage data.

I hope this step-by-step guide helps beginners gain confidence in working with the ABAP Dictionary. If you have any questions, suggestions, or additional tips, feel free to share them in the comments — I’d be happy to discuss and learn together.

If you found this helpful, feel free to connect with me.
I will be posting more beginner-friendly ABAP blogs regularly.

entry.title: Re: Bank Statement Item Not Marked as Completed After Aut...
entry.content:
Good day @Jessicaaguilar1 

Thank you for your question.

What you described is expected. Even if an open item is successfully cleared via an "external" clearing process, the bank statement item status is still determined by the bank statement processing lifecycle. The processing is handled in phases and statuses are set based on whether the bank statement item has completed the required processing steps in that flow.

Here  are answers to your questions:

Question: Is there any standard way for the system to automatically update the status of the bank statement item once the automatic clearing has been executed?

Answer: No, not automatically just because clearing happened elsewhere. In Public Cloud, the system typically does not change the bank statement item to completed merely because the related AR or AP item is cleared via a separate process. The item status is tied to the processing completion of bank statement.

Instead, automate the clearing within bank statement processing by using posting rules, processing rules and interpretations, so the item is posted and cleared during bank statement processing and then ends in Completed.

See these references for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/918bca53037f408f91a2295d04ac16bc/9929e3049c88402a8d56e437a3967e38.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/186460fdc35a4b64a713da9bb00deb1e/fee33bb4c897462886986b0294cec9a8.html?locale=en-US

 

Question: Is there any additional configuration for the item to be marked as completed without manual intervention?

Answer: There is no single switch that says "if cleared externally then mark statement item completed". What you can configure for true automation is the bank statement processing rule that is based on clearing so that when the statement is imported, the system performs the posting and clearing automatically. Then the item reaches a completed status as part of that flow.

See these reference for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/918bca53037f408f91a2295d04ac16bc/9929e3049c88402a8d56e437a3967e38.html?locale=en-US

Also, validate your posting or import setup:

If you import through parameter sets and thee method is "only import" but no posting, the statement may remain in a status that requires follow up processing.  See KBA 3320087 for more details on imported bank statement

 

Question: Is this behavior a standard limitation in SAP Public Cloud when the clearing is performed outside the bank statement processing flow?

Answer: Yes, that is the best way to describe it.

If clearing is executed outside the bank statement processing flow, the bank statement item can remain Not Completed and still required a "complete" action in the bank statement context. Which you can either manually or operationally control through the bank statement reprocessing app.

See these references for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/918bca53037f408f91a2295d04ac16bc/9929e3049c88402a8d56e437a3967e38.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/918bca53037f408f91a2295d04ac16bc/bc1fe656fe590950e10000000a44147b.html?locale=en-US

To summarise:

  • Clearing outside the bank statement flow does not necessarily set the bank statement item to Completed because status is tied to bank statement processing.
  • I recommend you use Reprocessing Bank Statement Items to check what step is missing and complete your process.

  • For true end to end automation, ensure clearing happens during bank statement processing by using rules and interpretation algorithms.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Automating Price Updates from MM Contracts into Task Lists (SAP PM): A Pragmatic Deep Dive
entry.content:
Business Problem

Maintenance organizations often run recurring plan/task list operations with service lines (ESLL). Prices are maintained centrally in MM service contracts (EKPO/ESLH/ESLL + PRS condition). Without automation, updating those prices across all task lists is a manual grind — and highly error‑prone.

Requirement: Get current contract prices into task list service lines without manually touching every ESLL row.

Solution: The report reads current PRS conditions (A081 → KONP) per LV leaf from the contract structure and updates the corresponding ESLL price rows in the maintenance task list — deterministically mapped via EXTROW.




What the Report Does (Functional Flow)

1) Discover the Contract’s LV Leaves

  • Resolve the root PACKNO from EKPO (PSTYP = 9).
  • Traverse the ESLL hierarchy using SUB_PACKNO and collect only leaf rows (non‑packages).
  • Extract for each leaf: PACKNO, INTROW, EXTROW, SRVPOS, KTEXT1.

Why leaves?
They’re the price‑bearing service lines that map 1:1 into the task list.

2) Read Current Conditions from A081/KONP

  • For each leaf, pull A081 using (KAPPL, KSCHL, KONT_PACK, KONT_ZEILE, WERKS, DATBI).
  • From KONP, read:
    • KBETR (unit price)
    • KONWA (currency)
    • KPEIN (price unit)
    • KMEIN (price unit UoM)

Build an internal price table keyed by EXTROW for fast lookup via binary search.

3) Load the Task List ESLL Rows

  • Use PLAS/PLPO → ESLH/ESLL (or MPOS as fallback) to fetch all relevant ESLL rows.
  • Bring currency context from ESLH-WAERS.
  • Optionally restrict by operation number VORNR.

4) Update Prices Consistently

Join logic

Match contract prices to task list lines via EXTROW.

Guardrails

  • Currency must match (KONWA == ESLH-WAERS).
  • PEINH and MENGE must be present.
  • If required data is missing → skip safely.

  • Pricing math

    NETWR/BRTWR = KBETR * (MENGE / PEINH)

    • Optional single price (TBTWR)

      Set only if units match (KMEIN == MEINS

      TBTWR = KBETR * (ESLL-PEINH / KONP-KPEIN)

      Optional MPOS header price

      MPOS-NETPR = KBETR

  • Modes

    • Simulation (default): no DB writes, full trace.
    • Real run: commits changes and summarizes results.

 

 

Why EXTROW Is the Right Mapping Key

  • Stability: More robust than internal row numbers when LV structures change.
  • Clarity: Business and IT can rely on one rule:
    “If EXTROW is copied from contract to task list, prices map 1:1.”
  • Performance: Enables sorting + binary search instead of fuzzy matching.



Key Technical Elements:

PARAMETERS: p_ebeln TYPE ekko-ebeln OBLIGATORY,   " Contract number
            p_ebelp TYPE ekpo-ebelp,              " Optional item
            p_werks TYPE werks_d    OBLIGATORY,   " Plant for A081
            p_kschl TYPE konp-kschl DEFAULT 'PRS',
            p_kappl TYPE konh-kappl DEFAULT 'MS'.

PARAMETERS: p_plnty TYPE plko-plnty DEFAULT 'A' OBLIGATORY, " Task list type
            p_plnnr TYPE plko-plnnr OBLIGATORY,             " Group
            p_plnal TYPE plko-plnal OBLIGATORY,             " Group counter
            p_vornr TYPE plpo-vornr.                        " Optional op

PARAMETERS: p_test   AS CHECKBOX DEFAULT 'X', " Simulation
            p_dbg    AS CHECKBOX DEFAULT 'X', " Debug/trace
            p_setmp  AS CHECKBOX,             " Set MPOS-NETPR
            p_settbw AS CHECKBOX DEFAULT 'X'. " Set ESLL-TBTWR​

 

Core Calculations

lv_scale     = gs_out_tl-menge / gs_out_tl-peinh.
lv_new_netwr = ls_price-kbetr * lv_scale.     " ESLL-NETWR/BRTWR

" Optional TBTWR (single price), only if units align:
IF ls_price-kmein = gs_out_tl-meins.
  lv_factor     = (gs_out_tl-peinh / ls_price-kpein) OR 1 if KPEIN = 0
  lv_tbtwr_new  = ls_price-kbetr * lv_factor.
ENDIF.

 

  • Built‑In Safeguards

    • Currency check (avoids accidental conversions).
    • Quantity/unit checks (avoid division by zero).
    • Unit congruence enforced for TBTWR.
    • Simulation mode gives trace + predictable dry-runs.
    • Counters summarize:
      • found leaves
      • A081 hits
      • KONP reads
      • task list lines
      • updates vs. skips
      • MPOS/TBTWR writes



Conclusion

The report solves a high‑friction maintenance problem with a precise, auditable mechanism:
it maps current contract prices into task list service lines using a stable external key (EXTROW), respects unit/currency safeguards, and allows safe simulation-first execution.

The full ABAP code can be found on my github: https://github.com/ProjectNautilus001/ABAP-Solutions-for-SAP-PM/blob/main/Y_PM_WPLAN_PREISUPDATE

entry.title: Introducing FormFlex: The Ultimate Dynamic Form Builder for SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Forms and data inputs are essential components in analytical dashboards, bridging the gap between passive data consumption and active user engagement. With the rising complexity of enterprise requirements, dashboard designers in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) frequently encounter limitations when attempting to build highly customizable, data-driven input forms without resorting to extensive scripting, hardcoded widgets, or external web pages.

To solve this challenge, we developed FormFlex—a powerful, robust, and highly dynamic custom widget designed to streamline form creation natively within SAP Analytics Cloud.

Unlike traditional static input fields which require developers to drag in fifty individual widgets, FormFlex operates on a configuration-driven architecture within a single widget wrapper. This enables users to generate fields dynamically through an intuitive visual Builder Panel. Whether you need a simple feedback survey, a complex data-entry wrapper for SAP HANA planning, or a configuration toggle panel, FormFlex seamlessly adapts to your needs.

Architecture & Features

Dynamic Field Engine

FormFlex natively binds to the following HTML5 input types without any required external polyfills:

  • Text: text, textarea, password, email
  • Numeric & Temporal: number, date, time, datetime-local, week
  • Boolean & Selection: checkbox, radio, select
  • System: color, submit

Fluid Grid Layouts

The form wrapper utilizes modern CSS Grid templates configurable directly from the Builder panel. By passing an integer (1-4), the widget instantaneously recalculates the responsive grid layout, removing the need for manual UI alignment.

Native SAP Theming Integration

One of the most frustrating aspects of using third-party Custom Widgets is realizing they break visually when a user changes their dashboard theme. FormFlex natively hooks into SAP Analytics Cloud's global CSS variables. Whether you are using the modern SAP Horizon theme, classic SAP Belize/Quartz, or toggling between Dark Mode, Light Mode, and High Contrast accessibility themes, FormFlex automatically restyles its background, borders, and typography to perfectly match the active SAP Canvas environment—zero configuration required!

Configuration Portability (Import & Export)

Building a complex form layout can be time-consuming, but with FormFlex, your work is never siloed. We introduced a robust Import and Export JSON pipeline directly into the Builder Panel. Dashboard administrators can click "Export Configuration" to download their exact UI, styling, and field configurations to their local hard drive. Later, they can share this JSON configuration with a colleague or upload it into a completely different dashboard widget, instantly cloning the form with a single click. The import pipeline even validates the schema structure to ensure compatibility!

How to Use FormFlex

Getting started with FormFlex is designed to be purely visual without writing any initial code. Here is the standard workflow:

  1. Installation: Download the FormFlex.json file and upload it to your SAP Analytics Cloud Custom Widgets portal.
  2. Add to Canvas: Drag the FormFlex widget onto your canvas alongside the FormFlex Builder Panel widget.
  3. Add Fields: In the Builder Panel, use the "Add Field (+)" button to dynamically generate your variables, labels, and input types (Text, Checkbox, Select, Date, Color, etc.).
  4. Configure Options: If you are using Select or Radio inputs, simply pass a comma-separated list of values (e.g. Option 1, Option 2, Option 3) directly in the builder.
  5. Reorder Visually: Use the ☰ drag handles on the left of each field row to HTML5 drag-and-drop the fields into your desired visual order.
  6. Style the Form: Adjust global aesthetics, background colors, borders, and column grid layouts inside the "Global Settings" and "Form Styling" accordion menus.

API Methods & Scripting

FormFlex exposes native methods to SAC's JavaScript runtime, allowing parent dashboards to interact with the widget programmatically.

1. getAllValues()

Retrieves the current state of all input fields inside the form. The data is returned as a serialized JSON string where the keys map to the Field ID.

// Example SAC Integration
var allData = FormFlex_1.getAllValues();
console.log(allData);

/*
returns all fields values
{
    "department_name": "IT",
    "skills": "SAP Analytics Cloud"
}
*/

2. getFormValue(fieldId)

Retrieves the value of a specific, single form field by its ID.

// Get just the department string
var dept = FormFlex_1.getFormValue("department_name");
console.log(dept);

3. setFormValue(fieldId, value)

Programmatically sets or overrides the value of a specific form field.

// Automatically check the terms agreeing checkbox
FormFlex_1.setFormValue("skills", "SAP Analytics Cloud");

//Form Multiple Sets (CheckBox) - seprate values with comma
FormFlex_1.setFormValue("skills", "SAP Analytics Cloud, SAP HANA");

4. setFormConfig(configJson)

Overlays or injects new configurations into specific form fields at runtime (e.g. dynamically changing labels, hiding inputs, or passing in completely new dynamic fields).

var newConfig = [
  {
    "id": "department_name",
    "type": "text",
    "label": "Department Name"
  },
  {
    "id": "Skills",
    "type": "text",
    "label": "Your Skills"
  }
];
FormFlex_1.setFormConfig(newConfig);

5. isButtonClicked(buttonId)

Validates whether a specific submit button clicked (if multiple buttons are present ). If there is only single button in form then you dont need this method, just write script in onSubmit() event. This method only works in onSubmit() event

if (FormFlex_1.isButtonClicked("btn_cancel")) {
    FormFlex_1.setFormValue("department_name", "");
    FormFlex_1.setFormValue("skills", "");
    Application.showMessage(ApplicationMessageType.Success, "Form Cleared!");
}

if (FormFlex_1.isButtonClicked("btn_data_action")) {
    var department = FormFlex_1.getFormValue("department_name");
    DataAction_1.setParameterValue("costcenter", department);
    DataAction_1.execute();
}

Events Hook

onSubmit()

This is the primary event hook triggered whenever a user clicks a button of type submit. Native HTML validation is checked automatically before this event fires.

// Inside the onSubmit() event of FormFlex_1
var allData = FormFlex_1.getAllValues();
var department = allData['department_name'];
DataAction_1.setParameterValue("costcenter", department);
DataAction_1.execute();

Resources

For the latest updates, documentation, or to contribute to the project, please visit the FormFlex GitHub Repository.

Conclusion

By migrating from statically unmanageable canvas widgets to FormFlex's highly scalable, configuration-driven architecture, SAP Analytics Cloud developers can ensure robust data collection and user-input tracking using standard JSON models. Stop hardcoding your forms today!

entry.title: Re: MASTER CONTRACT IN SALES CONTRACT
entry.content:
You can link a Sales Contract to a Master Contract as follows:

1) Create the Master Contract
Define overarching terms, validity, and framework conditions. This contract acts as the “parent” but doesn’t need customer/material details.

2) Create a Sales Contract
Enter customer, material, and commercial details as usual. In the contract header, locate the field Master Contract.

3) Reference the Master Contract ID
Populate the Master Contract field with the ID of the parent contract. Save the sales contract — now it is linked to the Master Contract.

System Behavior
The linkage is stored at header level. Reporting apps (e.g., “Manage Sales Contracts”) allow filtering and grouping by Master Contract. You can view all subordinate contracts together, regardless of customer or material differences.

entry.title: Driving Adoption with Improved Usability in SAP Sales & Service Cloud v2
entry.content:
User adoption remains one of the most critical success factors in any CRM initiative. Even the most powerful solution will struggle to deliver value if users find it complex, unintuitive, or disconnected from their daily work.

With SAP Sales & Service Cloud version 2, usability is no longer a secondary consideration — it is a core capability. The solution offers a wide range of features that allow organizations to adapt, personalize, and automate the user experience, helping sales and service teams work more efficiently and with greater confidence.

This blog highlights the key usability pillars covered in the SAP Sales & Service Cloud v2 – Usability Playbook and shows how they can help organizations move from basic adoption to meaningful business impact.

Why Usability Deserves Strategic Attention

Usability directly influences how users perceive a system, how quickly they become productive, and how consistently they engage with it over time. Across customer projects, we consistently see that usability-focused initiatives lead to:

  • Faster onboarding of new users
  • Higher engagement in daily activities
  • Improved confidence when executing sales and service processes
  • Stronger alignment between business processes and system behavior

Rather than relying solely on training or change management, organizations can achieve better outcomes by designing the system to fit the user, not the other way around.

First Impressions Matter: System Preferences and Branding

The user experience begins long before the first opportunity or service ticket is created. Visual consistency and thoughtful system behavior play an important role in user perception.

SAP Sales & Service Cloud v2 allows organizations to configure system preferences such as:

  • Corporate branding through themes, logos, and login images
  • Clean background screens to reduce visual clutter
  • Session behavior settings like auto sign‑out for security and compliance

These configurations are low effort but create familiarity, reinforce trust, and help users feel more connected to the system from day one.

A Home Page That Guides Users, Not Distracts Them

The home page is where users start their day — and where they decide what to do next. In SAP Sales & Service Cloud v2, the home page can be adapted to become a true productivity hub.

Key capabilities include:

  • Action‑oriented cards such as tasks, opportunities, pipeline insights, and learning content
  • Personalized layouts that reflect different roles and priorities
  • Visual customization with background images and greeting cards

When users see relevant information immediately after logging in, they spend less time searching and more time acting.

Everyday Productivity Through Smart UI Features

Many of the most powerful usability features in SAP Sales & Service Cloud v2 are available out of the box and require no activation. These features significantly reduce navigation effort and context switching.

Examples include:

  • Favorites for one‑click access to frequently used objects
  • Quick View to preview and update information without leaving the current screen
  • Inline and mass editing in lists
  • Multi‑tab grouping for users handling multiple accounts or cases
  • Direct navigation URLs for deep linking from emails or collaboration tools
  • Document Flow to visualize end‑to‑end business processes

Together, these capabilities help users stay focused and productive throughout the day.

Adaptation: Designing the System for the Business

Adaptation capabilities allow administrators to tailor the application to business needs while maintaining governance and consistency.

Key adaptation features include:

  • Managing and reordering fields and sections
  • Creating custom tabs and buttons
  • Adding extension fields and mashups
  • Defining dynamic property rules and page layouts
  • Applying layout assignment rules based on roles or conditions

These tools enable organizations to align the system with real processes instead of forcing users to adapt to a generic interface.

Personalization: Empowering Users Without Losing Control

While adaptation is typically administrator‑driven, personalization puts flexibility directly in the hands of business users.

Users can:

  • Show, hide, or reorder fields
  • Create and personalize filters
  • Customize tabs and sections
  • Rearrange home page cards

This balance between structured design and individual flexibility increases satisfaction and encourages long‑term adoption.

Working Where Users Already Are: Outlook, Gmail, and Teams

Productivity improves when CRM integrates seamlessly with daily tools. SAP Sales & Service Cloud v2 offers deep integration with:

  • Microsoft Outlook and Gmail for emails, tasks, contacts, appointments, and visits
  • Microsoft Teams for collaboration, data sharing, and contextual access

Users can collaborate, review information, and take action without constantly switching applications — a key factor for sustained engagement.

Supporting Users with Automation and Guidance

Beyond interface design, usability is also about reducing friction and manual effort. The playbook highlights additional features that support this goal, such as:

  • Built‑In Support for self‑service troubleshooting
  • In‑app guidance with WalkMe (optional)
  • Global search optimization
  • Document Library for structured content management
  • Duplicate checks to improve data quality
  • Autoflow and approvals for process automation
  • Business alerts to keep users informed

These capabilities help users work more confidently while maintaining process integrity.

Explore the Full Usability Playbook

This blog offers a snapshot of what is possible with SAP Sales & Service Cloud v2. The full Usability Playbook goes deeper, with visual examples, configuration guidance, and practical recommendations that can be applied across sales and service scenarios.

If you are involved in CRM design, implementation, or adoption initiatives, the playbook can serve as a valuable reference to help you create experiences that users genuinely appreciate.

Download the SAP Sales & Service Cloud v2 – Usability Playbook and explore how usability can become a strategic advantage in your CRM journey.

entry.title: Re: Billing based on sales orders without billing plan
entry.content:
• If you want billing without a billing plan but still deliveries, TAN is the right choice — but billing will always depend on delivery.
  • If you want billing directly from the order without delivery, you must use TAS.
  • There is no special configuration in Public Cloud to make TAN behave like TAS. The distinction is intentional and enforced.

entry.title: Smallest OData MCP Server in the World!
entry.content:
Hello,

I have build the smallest OData MCP server (in the world 😁)!

You can find the source code (publicly available) here:

  • https://github.com/danmcool/odata_mcpsrv_xs
  • DISCLAIMER: current authentication method used is only for test purposes, for a production environment service to service authentication has to be implemented!

Why this is useful?

  • ChatGPT proposes a new BETA feature (DEVELOPER mode only) from September 2025 that removes the need to implement an AI Agent in order to retrieve live data from an application, we can directly connect a MCP server!
  • ChatGPT is able to understand OData (how parameters/filters work, creates an API call plan, etc), navigates through errors, and finds the right API to query, and this is amazing, as it is based only on the service metadata!

What does that mean:

  • You can query your OData SAP endpoints using natural language and retrieve live data from your applications.
  • Do not expect instantaneous responses.

Let me know what you think!

Best regards

entry.title: Re: Settlement periods & Billing Date period of service c...
entry.content:
Why setting item quantity to 6 months multiplies the billing value

In the service contract, the item quantity directly drives the billing value. If you set the quantity to “6” (months), the system interprets that as six units of the price condition, so the total value is multiplied by six. That’s why you saw €36,000 instead of €6,000 — the system is not “accumulating” months, it’s simply multiplying the base condition. This is expected behavior. The billing plan itself does not change the contract item quantity; it only schedules billing dates and amounts.

What “Periodic Billing Plans” mean

  • When I mentioned periodic billing, I was referring to billing plan rules in customizing, not just the billing date field.
    • Billing date in the plan: This is the calendar date when the invoice is due.
    • Billing rule / settlement period in customizing: This defines how the system interprets the billing period (monthly, quarterly, half‑yearly).
      • For example, with a half‑yearly rule, the system can accumulate six months of service into one billing date.
      • This requires the correct billing rule assignment in the item category customizing (e.g., “periodic billing” vs. “milestone billing”).
  • In Public Cloud, customizing options are limited compared to on‑premise, but the principle is:
    • The billing plan date controls when you bill.
    • The billing rule controls how much you bill (e.g., accumulate periods vs. single unit).

entry.title: upload pictures?
entry.content:
In Step 5c of "Enhance the Business Object Data Model and Enable OData Streams" (https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/abap-environment-rap100-enhance-data-model.html), you should be able to upload an image, but even though I have written "@Semantics.largeObject" in STEP 2, the Upload File button does not appear when I open the Travel entity.

CDS data definition

@AccessControl.authorizationCheck: #MANDATORY
@Metadata.allowExtensions: true
@ObjectModel.sapObjectNodeType.name: 'ZRAP100_ATRAV90B'
@EndUserText.label: '###GENERATED Core Data Service Entity'
define root view entity ZR_RAP100_ATRAV90B
  as select from zrap100_atrav90b as Travel

  association [0..1] to /DMO/I_Agency            as _Agency        on $projection.AgencyID = _Agency.AgencyID
  association [0..1] to /DMO/I_Customer          as _Customer      on $projection.CustomerID = _Customer.CustomerID
  association [1..1] to /DMO/I_Overall_Status_VH as _Overallstatus on $projection.OverallStatus = _Overallstatus.OverallStatus
  association [0..1] to I_Currency               as _Currency      on $projection.CurrencyCode = _Currency.Currency
{
  key travel_id             as TravelID,
      agency_id             as AgencyID,
      customer_id           as CustomerID,
      begin_date            as BeginDate,
      end_date              as EndDate,
      @Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'CurrencyCode'
      booking_fee           as BookingFee,
      @Semantics.amount.currencyCode: 'CurrencyCode'
      total_price           as TotalPrice,
      @Consumption.valueHelpDefinition: [ {
        entity.name: 'I_CurrencyStdVH',
        entity.element: 'Currency',
        useForValidation: true
      } ]
      currency_code         as CurrencyCode,
      description           as Description,
      overall_status        as OverallStatus,
      @Semantics.largeObject: { mimeType: 'MimeType',   //case-sensitive
                             fileName: 'FileName',   //case-sensitive
                             acceptableMimeTypes: ['image/png', 'image/jpeg'],
                             contentDispositionPreference: #ATTACHMENT }
      attachment            as Attachment,
      @Semantics.mimeType: true
      mime_type             as MimeType,
      file_name             as FileName,
      @Semantics.user.createdBy: true
      created_by            as CreatedBy,
      @Semantics.systemDateTime.createdAt: true
      created_at            as CreatedAt,
      @Semantics.user.localInstanceLastChangedBy: true
      local_last_changed_by as LocalLastChangedBy,
      @Semantics.systemDateTime.localInstanceLastChangedAt: true
      local_last_changed_at as LocalLastChangedAt,
      @Semantics.systemDateTime.localInstanceLastChangedAt: true
      last_changed_at       as LastChangedAt,

      // public assosiations
      _Customer,
      _Agency,
      _Overallstatus,
      _Currency
}

entry.title: Re: WFA on HANA template
entry.content:
Hi Alex,

You can find the WFA on HANA information in the following wiki page.

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – Adding a GUI transaction to the launchpad in SAP S/4HANA 2023 or hig
entry.content:
Thanks @zfiori !

If you have a lot of transactions to bring in  - e.g. 100 or more - for efficiency, I suggest: 

  1. Group up your custom transactions by functional area and review your Semantic Object options in advance - you can see the full list in transaction /UI2/SEMOBJ_SAP and filter by application component 
  2. Agree your naming convention for actions up front
  3. Do NOT adjust the navigation parameters in the first round - get the transactions into the role first. You can always refine the navigation parameters later. 
  4. Keep all transactions for the same functional work stream in the same technical catalog. It’s fine to have up to 100 GUi transactions in the same technical catalog.
  5. Use the mass tools for assigning business catalogs and roles such as program PRGN_CREATE_FIORIFRONTENDROLE  where you can.

entry.title: Re: Statistical Sales Condition in Margin Analysis should...
entry.content:
How the system interprets debit vs. credit in COPA
COPA does not “know” whether a statistical condition is a cost or revenue by its nature. It simply transfers the value from billing with the sign defined in the condition type.

  • Positive sign → treated as revenue/favorable.
  • Negative sign → treated as cost/unfavorable.

The statistical flag only prevents the condition from affecting net price in SD; it does not influence how COPA interprets the value. So, if your condition type is defined with a positive sign, COPA will always show it as favorable, even if you conceptually intend it to be a cost.

Why contribution margin is not reduced in extension ledger 0C
Because the condition type is set up with a positive sign, the value flows into COPA as revenue. Contribution margin is calculated as revenue minus cost. Since your statistical condition is not flagged as a cost (negative sign), it does not reduce the margin. The system does not apply semantic logic (“this is a cost condition”) — it only follows the sign definition.

What you need to adjust
In the pricing condition type configuration, set the Plus/Minus field to negative. This ensures the value is transferred to COPA as a cost.

Condition Category - If the condition is meant to represent product cost, assign the correct Condition Category (e.g., G, S, or T). This ensures the system pulls the right valuation basis (standard cost, moving average, etc.).

Pricing Procedure
Verify the condition is marked as statistical in the pricing procedure. Ensure the requirement (e.g., Requirement 4 – Cost) is correctly assigned so the system interprets it as a cost element.

Dear All,

In an SAP IQ MPX environment, increased IQ_SYSTEM_MAIN usage has occurred.
Monitoring results: Increased 'X' BlockType.

IQ_SYSTEM_MAIN Usage : 33%, BIkTypes : 2H,13421760F,32D,63334A, 2659794O,5123238X, 128M,147491B,32C,89857G
IO_SYSTEM_MAIN Usage : 35%, BIkTypes : 2H,13421760F,32D,260A,2663956O,6849212X,128M,159309B,32C,165057G
IQ_SYSTEM_MAIN Usage : 47%, BIkTypes : 2H,13421760F,32D,212A,2661486O,14929082X,128M,178055B,32C,165471G
IQ_SYSTEM_MAIN Usage : 60%, BIkTypes : 2H,13421760F,32D,288A,2661728O,23676034X,128M,175249B,32C,165348G
IQ_SYSTEM_MAIN Usage : 72%, BIkTypes : 2H,13421760F,32D,306A,2665915O,31245236X,128M,175597B,32C,166299G
IO_SYSTEM MAIN Usage : 84%, BIkTypes : 2H,13421760F,32D,42430A,2667623O,39787999X,128M,172528B,32C,166582G

entry.title: Re: Output Determination Management - Send out 2 emails w...
entry.content:
Good day @Stanvh 

Thank you for your question.

Yes, this is possible. If you configure Output Parameter Determination to create multiple output items for the same PO. OPD can result in a single or many output items. This however, depends on how your decision tables are set up.

See the following reference for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a630d57fc5004c6383e7a81efee7a8bb/fdc3cf565b1b6b15e10000000a441470.html?locale=en-US

I recommend you:

  • Create a second output type for example Z_SHIPPING_LABEL.
  • In Output Type determination, switch the decision table hit policy to return all matching rules, then maintain two matching rows for Purchasing Org = P010. One for PURCHASE_ORDER, one for Z_SHIPPING_LABEL.
  • Keep "CP" as receiver and channel = Emails for both output types.
  • Maintain a different email templates and different form templates per output type.

This will enable you generate two output items and therefore send two separate emails. One for your PO and the other for your Shipping Label.

This Blog provides good guidance:

How to generate multiple output items for production orders with different form templates

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Automate Documents End-to-End with SAP Document AI (Beginner’s Guide)
entry.content:
Introduction:

SAP Document AI is a Business Technology Platform (BTP) service designed to automate extraction of information from diverse business documents, such as invoices, regardless of their layout or content structure. It is available in Cloud Foundry and Kyma environments. To set up SAP Document AI in a free BTP trial account, users need an S-user ID to create a trial BTP Trial global account and sub-account. Check this link for more information.

Below are the setup steps and example about Sap Document AI-

SAP Document AI is available in the following environments:

  • Cloud Foundry Environment
  • Kyma environment

 Setup steps:

Get started with SAP Document AI using the standard procedures for SAP BTP Cloud Foundry environment or Kyma environment.

Prerequisites to set up SAP Document AI in BTP trial account-

1.Required S-user id for BTP Trial account creation.

 

BTP Trial account login screen

 

2.Select the Region and create Sub-account in BTP Global account

 

3.In Sub-account add the “SAP document AI” Subscription and instance.

After adding the instance and subscription of SAP Document AI. Go to Subscription three dots and click on Go to Application Option.

Example- Now you will get the SAP Document AI subscription click this then UI will open. Here you will get the option to upload the invoice.

Below steps for uploading documents in SAP Document AI -

  1. In the top right, click + (Upload Document).

2.On the Select Document screen, choose the document type from drop down list -Invoice, Payment Advice, Purchase Order, Custom.

3.Next, select the Schema.

You must select a schema when you add documents. By default, SAP Document AI proposes the appropriate preconfigured SAP schema for your document type. To see the header and line-item fields that are available in an SAP schema, choose the desired Schema on the Select Document screen, and then choose Extraction Fields to display the schema details.

For Upload the document in this page click on + icon and upload the invoice add the document from system and click on confirm.

In Below screenshot document type is Invoice and Schema type is SAP Invoice Schema.

After uploading Save and Confirm check the status is pending or done of uploaded document.

After successful upload of the document in “SAP Document AI” data extraction process will start, and it will show the extraction confidence range like red, yellow and green status with accuracy percentage.

Handling Warnings and Errors in Invoice Data:

The invoice below displays warnings and errors in the data. SAP Document AI allows you to easily identify these issues, providing an efficient way to review and correct errors and warnings in extracted invoice information. This feature enhances the analysis and accuracy of invoice data by enabling users to update any incorrect entries after extraction.

Warning data- Click on Extraction warning confidence range and check the warning data if required changes update and confirm.

When the extracted data from the invoice is updated correctly, it is indicated by a green status. This green status signifies that the information has been accurately captured and no further corrections are necessary, confirming the integrity and reliability of the invoice data.

Error in Invoice-

Errors may occur in invoices during the document upload and extraction process in SAP Document AI. These errors can impact the accuracy and reliability of the extracted data, making it necessary to review and correct them to ensure proper invoice processing.

When you add documents, you need to choose a schema. SAP Document AI will automatically suggest a suitable SAP schema based on your document type. To view the available header and line-item fields in the chosen schema, simply select the Schema on the Select Document screen, and then click on Extraction Fields to see the details.

Reviewing and Updating Errors or Warnings

If the uploaded invoice contains errors or warnings, check them in the Extraction Confidence Range. The errors and warnings are indicated by specific color codes. Make the necessary changes to the invoice data and save your updates. Once saved, the invoice data and the confidence range color codes for errors and warnings will be updated to reflect the corrections.

Correcting Invoice Information

For example, in the invoice below, the Bill To section displays the Client Name and Address as not correctly updated in the Extraction Confidence Range. This indicates that these fields require review and adjustment to ensure accurate data extraction and confidence scoring.

Steps to Correct Invoice Data:

Step1: Go to edit mode.

When the edit button is selected, all the invoice data will change to a gray shade. This indicates that the fields are now in editable mode, allowing you to make the necessary corrections.

Step 2: Click on the Name and Address fields in the invoice.

These fields can now be updated as needed to correct any errors that were identified during the data extraction process. Enter the correct field value and then click on "Apply" to save your changes to that field.

Once you have saved and confirmed your changes, the error will be resolved. The extraction value will be updated and displayed in green, indicating that the correction has been successfully applied and the data is now considered accurate.

Conclusion: 

This document outlines the process for identifying and correcting errors in uploaded invoice data using the Extraction Confidence Range. SAP Document AI is smart tool for organizations to automate document processing with accuracy and speed, reducing manual effort and errors. By leveraging AI and seamless SAP integration, it given smarter, scalable, and more efficient business processes.
