entry.title: SAP CI to ABAP Integration: Seamless XML to Internal Table Mapping
entry.content:
In many SAP CI integrations, XML responses are received from external systems and passed to the ABAP layer. A common requirement is to convert this XML data into an internal table in ABAP.

There are two common approaches:

  1. Use XSLT mapping in SAP Backend (SAP ECC/S4HANA) to transform the XML into the required structure. ( You can follow blog https://community.sap.com/t5/application-development-and-automation-blog-posts/xslt-transformations-xml-2-abap-a-working-example-with-deep-structures/ba-p/13370654 )
  2. Prepare the XML in SAP CI in ABAP XML format so that it can be directly converted into an internal table using ABAP transformation.

This blog explains the second and simpler approach.

Why Use ABAP XML Format?

If the XML follows the ABAP XML structure with the required tags:

  • <abap version="1.0">
  • <values>

Then ABAP can directly deserialize the XML into an internal table using:

CALL TRANSFORMATION id

This avoids complex XSLT mappings and simplifies the integration.

Sample XML That Can Be Directly Converted

Below is a sample XML payload prepared in SAP CI that can be directly converted into an internal table in ABAP:

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<abap version="1.0">
    <values>
        <OUTPUT>
            <ZCON_PAC_LOCATION>
                <LOCATIONCODE>US</LOCATIONCODE>
                <PXCREATEDATETIME>2010-01-29 15:44:01</PXCREATEDATETIME>
                <PXCREATEOPNAME>Fname Lname</PXCREATEOPNAME>
                <PXCREATEOPERATOR>test@mail.com</PXCREATEOPERATOR>
            </ZCON_PAC_LOCATION>
        </OUTPUT>
    </values>
</abap>

Important: The tags <abap version="1.0"> and <values> are mandatory for ABAP deserialization using the ID transformation.

Generating the XSD

The simplest way to generate the XSD is:

  1. Create an RFC-enabled function module with the required ABAP structure.
  2. Access the WSDL using: http://<host>:<port>/sap/bc/soap/wsdl11?services=<RFC_Name>&sap-client=<client>
  3. Copy the relevant schema portion from the WSDL.

Sample XSD

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<xs:schema xmlns:xs="http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema"
           xmlns:asx="http://www.sap.com/abapxml"
           xmlns:sap="http://www.sap.com/sapxsl"
           elementFormDefault="qualified"
           attributeFormDefault="unqualified">

    <xs:element name="abap" type="AbapType"/>

    <xs:complexType name="AbapType">
        <xs:sequence>
            <xs:element name="values" type="ValuesType"/>
        </xs:sequence>
        <xs:attribute name="version" type="xs:string" use="required"/>
    </xs:complexType>

    <xs:complexType name="ValuesType">
        <xs:sequence>
            <xs:element name="OUTPUT" type="OutputType"/>
        </xs:sequence>
    </xs:complexType>

    <xs:complexType name="OutputType">
        <xs:sequence>
            <xs:element name="ZCN_PAC_LOCATION"
                        type="PacLocation"
                        minOccurs="0"
                        maxOccurs="unbounded"/>
        </xs:sequence>
    </xs:complexType>

    <xs:complexType name="PacLocation">
        <xs:sequence>
            <xs:element name="LOCATIONCODE" type="xs:string" minOccurs="0"/>
            <xs:element name="PXCREATEDATETIME" type="xs:string" minOccurs="0"/>
            <xs:element name="PXCREATEOPNAME" type="xs:string" minOccurs="0"/>
            <xs:element name="PXCREATEOPERATOR" type="xs:string" minOccurs="0"/>
        </xs:sequence>
    </xs:complexType>
</xs:schema>

SAP CI Mapping:


In SAP CI, map the response so that:

  • The root node is abap with attribute version="1.0".
  • Under it, include values.
  • Under values, include your wrapper node such as OUTPUT.
  • Inside the wrapper, repeat the structure node for each record.

Once structured correctly, no additional transformation is required in ABAP.

ABAP code :

"Data Declarations
DATA: lo_http_client TYPE REF TO if_http_client,
      lv_request     TYPE xstring,
      lv_response    TYPE xstring.
"Convert Internal Table to XML (Request to CPI)
CALL TRANSFORMATION id
  SOURCE pac_project_department_request = lt_request[]
  RESULT XML lv_request.

"Create HTTP Client
cl_http_client=>create_by_destination(
    EXPORTING
        destination              = 'DestinationName'
    IMPORTING
        client                   = lo_http_client
    EXCEPTIONS
        argument_not_found       = 1
        destination_not_found    = 2
        destination_no_authority = 3
        plugin_not_active        = 4
        internal_error           = 5
        OTHERS                   = 6 ).

IF sy-subrc <> 0.
"Suitable error handling
ENDIF.
"Disable logon popup:
lo_http_client->propertytype_logon_popup = lo_http_client->co_disabled.
"Send HTTP Request
lo_http_client->request->set_method( 'POST' ).
"Set xml request body
lo_http_client->request->set_data( data = lv_request ).
lo_http_client->send(
    EXCEPTIONS
        http_communication_failure = 1
        http_invalid_state         = 2
        http_processing_failed     = 3
        http_invalid_timeout       = 4
        OTHERS                     = 5 ).

IF sy-subrc = 0.
    lo_http_client->receive(
        EXCEPTIONS
            http_communication_failure = 1
            http_invalid_state         = 2
            http_processing_failed     = 3
            OTHERS                     = 5 ).
ENDIF.
lo_http_client->close( ).
lv_response = lo_http_client->response->get_data( ).
"Convert XML Response to Internal Table
CALL TRANSFORMATION id
  SOURCE XML lv_response
  RESULT output = et_pac_departments.

ABAP Dictionary Objects

  • Internal table: ET_PAC_DEPARTMENTS
  • Table type: ZCON_PAC_DEPARTMENT_TY
  • Line type: ZCON_PAC_DEPARTMENT

The internal table ET_PAC_DEPARTMENTS is defined with table type ZCON_PAC_DEPARTMENT_TY.

The table type ZCON_PAC_DEPARTMENT_TY refers to the line structure ZCON_PAC_DEPARTMENT.

The structure ZCON_PAC_DEPARTMENT contains the following fields:

  • LOCATIONCODE (TYPE string)
  • PXCREATEDATETIME (TYPE string)
  • PXCREATEOPNAME (TYPE string)
  • PXCREATEOPERATOR (TYPE string)

Since the XML structure matches the ABAP structure exactly, the CALL TRANSFORMATION id statement automatically deserializes the XML and populates the internal table.

 

Conclusion

By preparing the XML payload in SAP CI using ABAP XML format with the correct structure and mandatory tags, we can directly convert the XML into an internal table in ABAP using CALL TRANSFORMATION id.

This approach simplifies SAP CI integration scenarios and reduces transformation complexity significantly.

entry.title: Detailed process overview of Outbound ASR Process in TM - Warehouse driven process
entry.content:
Advanced Shipping and Receiving (ASR) in SAP TM

From TU-Centric Integration to a Freight Order–Driven Model

Why ASR Matters?

In many SAP TM–EWM landscapes, Transportation Units (TUs) have traditionally driven warehouse execution. While proven, this approach introduces object redundancy, synchronization challenges, and rigidity when transportation plans change.

Advanced Shipping and Receiving (ASR) changes this by making the SAP TM Freight Order the single orchestration object across transportation and warehouse execution.

 

What Is ASR?

Advanced Shipping and Receiving (ASR) is an end-to-end logistics process in SAP S/4HANA that enables shipping, receiving, and goods movement using a single Freight Order, even across multiple warehouses.

With ASR:

  • One Freight Order can handle multiple loading and unloading points
  • Truck arrival, loading, unloading, and goods movement are coordinated centrally
  • Warehouse execution aligns with Freight Order events, not TUs

 ASR is especially powerful for multi-stop, milk-run, and dynamic planning scenarios.

 

 

Traditional vs ASR – What Changed?

 

TRADITIONAL TU-BASED INTEGRATION

 

ASR INTEGRATION MODEL

 

Traditional Approach: In TU-based integration, EWM manages transportation units (TUs) for warehouse processes.

 

Integration Approach: ASR is based on the SAP TM freight order (FO) and doesn’t use the EWM transportation unit (TU) or the EWM vehicle.

 

Object Focus: EWM TUs are used for planning, execution, and tracking of goods within the warehouse.

 

Object Focus: ASR revolves around the freight order as the central object for planning, packaging, and loading.

 

Benefits: Well-established processes, especially suited for complex warehouse scenarios.

 

Benefits: Streamlines communication, reduces redundancy, and enhances visibility across TM and EWM systems.

 

Constraints: May involve additional complexity due to TU management and synchronization between TM and EWM.

 

Constraints: ASR will not work in Standalone system.

 

When to Use TU vs ASR

Use TU-Based Integration When:

  • Transportation plans are stable
  • Physical TU tracking is mandatory
  • Warehouse processes are simple

Use ASR When:

  • One Freight Order spans multiple warehouses
  • Dock and yard capacity drive execution
  • Transportation plans change frequently
  • You need one cargo view and unified status

Rule of thumb:
If time slots and capacity matter more than physical TUs, ASR is the better fit.

Warehouse-Driven vs Transportation-Driven Process

Understanding the execution driver is essential when deciding between traditional integration and ASR.

🔹Warehouse-Driven Process

In a warehouse-driven process:

  • Warehouse activities (picking, packing, staging) drive the flow

  • Transportation is triggered after warehouse readiness

  • Focus is on internal warehouse efficiency

🔹Transportation-Driven Process (ASR Model)

In a transportation-driven process:

  • Execution is initiated and controlled by the Freight Order in TM

  • Delivery will be blocked for transportation activities
  • Warehouse activities are started only after the notification from the freight order

Key Difference in One Line

In a warehouse-driven process, the warehouse dictates transportation timing.
In a transportation-driven (ASR) process, transportation dictates warehouse execution.

 

Core Functionalities of ASR

  • Freight Order–centric execution
  • Unified inbound and outbound process handling
  • End-to-end visibility across TM and EWM
  • Reduced business object duplication
  • Simplified replanning with minimal warehouse disruption

ASR is not just an integration change — it is process harmonization.

 

Configuration & Master Data Setup

  • Advanced Shipping and Receiving (ASR) relies on tight integration between ERP, EWM, and TM.
  • The following configurations are scenario-critical, meaning ASR will not function correctly without them.
  • Instead of listing all possible settings, this section highlights only those configurations that directly impact ASR execution.

 

PRE-REQUISITES:

  • Activate BC Sets- Inbound and Outbound

 

  • Inbound Delivery BC Set:

Activate BC Set:  /SCWM/DLV_INBOUND_FO for EWM document type IFO

 

  • Enable the item type IDFO for the use of serial numbers

In Customizing, choose SCM Extended Warehouse Management → Extended Warehouse Management → Cross-Process Settings → Delivery – Warehouse Request → Quantity Offsetting → Define Quantity Offsetting Profiles

 

  • Select the profile „/SCWM/INB_PRD_DLV_FO” and choose the view “Assignment of Quantity Roles” on the left side.
  • For the quantity role “SN”, deselect the “Inactive” checkbox.

 

Map Document Type from ERP System to EWM Document Type IFO

               DocTypeERP - EL

               Doc. Type     -    IFO

Map Item Type from ERP System to EWM Item Type IDFO

               DocTypeERP - EL

               ItmTpERP    -      ELN

               Doc. Type         IFO

               Item Type         IDFO

Now you need to do determination of WPT according to your Doc type and Item type

 

Activate BC Set /SCWM/DLV_INBOUND_PACK

 

If you want to automatically create packaging items for the packaging materials of HUs in the EWM inbound delivery, you need to activate this BC set. This BC set includes the EWM item type IPHU.

 

Automatically Generate Packaging Items for EWM Inbound Delivery:

 

In Customizing, choose SCM Extended Warehouse Management → Extended Warehouse Management → Goods Receipt Process → Inbound Delivery → Automatically Generate Packaging Items for Inbound Deliveries.

 

               Warehouse Number                 ZS01   

               Doc. Cat.                                        PDI

               Doc. Type                                       IFO

               Dlv. Item creation                       X

               Implicit GR                                    X

               Locking During Creation                        Blank

 

Open the view “Relevance of Packmaterials” and select the packaging type for the delivery item creation, for example, packing material type ‘PALT’.

 

Open the view „Determination of Delivery Item Type“, and enter the following data:

               Packaging Material Type         PALT

               Warehouse Number                 ZS01

               Doc. Cat.                                        PDI

               Doc. Type                                       IFO

               Item Type                                       IPHU

               Stock Type                                     F1

Define Delivery Item Category Determination for the Usage of Automatically Generated Packaging Items in EWM Inbound Delivery

 

In Customizing, choose Logistics Execution → Shipping → Deliveries → Define Item Category Determination in Deliveries.

               Delivery Type                                EL

               Item Category Group                VERP

               Usage                                              Blank

               ItemCat - HighLevItm                             Blank

               Default Item Category                            ELP

Add Transaction Code to Movement Type for the Usage of Automatically Generated Packaging Item in EWM Inbound Delivery

 

If you want to automatically create packaging items for the packaging materials of HUs in EWM inbound delivery, you need to perform the following Customizing activity. This setting is necessary for the goods receipt reversal process:

 

  1. In Customizing, choose Materials Management → Inventory Management and Physical Inventory → Movement Types → Copy, Change Movement types.
  2. Select the „Movement type“checkbox”.
  3. Enter the selection range from movement type 502 to movement type 502.
  4. Select the view “Allowed Transactions” and add the transaction code VL09.

 

 

  • OUTBOUND DELIVERY BC SET

 Activate BC Set /SCWM/DLV_OUTBOUND_FO  for EWM Document Type OFO

  1. From the initial screen, enter transaction SCPR20 or /NSCPR20.
  2. Enter /SCWM/DLV_OUTBOUND_FO in the BC SET field and choose “Activate BC Set” (F7). Check the BCS documentation and adapt the settings for your process. Enable the Item Type ODFO for the Use of Serial Numbers

If you use materials with a serial number requirement in EWM in the outbound process, you need to enable the item type ODFO for the use of serial numbers.

  1. In Customizing, choose SCM Extended Warehouse Management → Extended Warehouse Management → Cross-Process Settings → Delivery – Warehouse Request → Quantity Offsetting → Define Quantity Offsetting Profiles.
  2. Select the profile /SCWM/OUT_PRD_DLV_FO and choose the view „Assignment of Quantity Roles“ on the left side.
  3. For the quantity role “SN”, deselect the “Inactive” checkbox. Define Process Profile for Document Header

You can enable the immediate quantity update to the LE delivery for quantity changes in the outbound delivery order, and immediate handling unit update to the LE delivery with the creation of the EWM outbound delivery.

In Customizing, choose SCM Extended Warehouse Management → Extended Warehouse Management → Cross-Process Settings → Delivery – Warehouse Request → Process Management and Control → Define Process Profile for Document Header.

Enable immediate quantity update

  1. Select profile /SCWM/OUT_PRD_FO, and choose “‘Details”.
  2. Select the checkbox for ‘Quantity Update’.

Enable immediate handling unit update

  1. Select profile /SCWM/OUT_FD_FO and choose “Details”.
  2. Select the checkbox for ‘HU Update’.

Map Document Type from ERP System to EWM Document Type OFO

  1. In Customizing, choose SCM Extended Warehouse Management → Extended Warehouse Management → Interfaces → ERP Integration → Delivery Processing → Map Document Types from ERP System to EWM.
  2. Map ERP document type LF to EWM document type OFO.

               DocTypeERP                  LF

               Doc. Type                        OFO

Map Item Type from ERP System to EWM Item Type ODFO

  1. In Customizing, choose SCM Extended Warehouse Management → Extended Warehouse Management → Interfaces → ERP Integration → Delivery Processing → Map Item Types from ERP System to EWM.
  2. Map ERP document type LF, ERP item type TAN, and EWM document type OFO to EWM item type ODFO.

               DocTypeERP   LF

               ItmTpERP          TAN

               Doc. Type         OFO

               Item Type         ODFO

Determine the WPY based on your Doc Type

Maintain POD Relevance

  1. In Customizing, choose Logistics Execution → Shipping → Deliveries → Proof of Delivery → Set POD-Relevance Depending on Delivery Item Category.

               Item Category               TAN

               POD-relevant V (Copy POD relevance from preceding document)

               POD automatic            blank

SCENARIO SPECIFIC CONFIGURATIONS

Map Delivery Type – Document Type

Mapping delivery types from an ERP system to document types in EWM ensures that when a delivery document is created in the ERP system (such as a sales order or purchase order), it is correctly interpreted and processed in EWM and TM. The mapping acts as a translation layer between systems, aligning document semantics and enabling automated processing.

 

Responsible

 

SAP Module

TM

Customizing Path

IMG->SCM Extended Warehouse Management->Extended Warehouse Management->Interfaces->ERP Integration->Delivery Processing->Map Document Types from ERP System to EWM

 

IMG Transaction

SPRO

Dependency

Client Dependent

Related Tables

 

 

 

Mapping of ERP Item Type

Mapping ERP item types to EWM document types ensures accurate item-level processing in SAP TM-EWM integration.
This configuration links ERP item categories with corresponding EWM document structures.
It supports seamless logistics execution by aligning item semantics across systems.
Key settings include business system, item type, client dependency, and change tracking.

 

Responsible

 

SAP Module

TM

Customizing Path

IMG->SCM Extended Warehouse Management->Extended Warehouse Management->Interfaces->ERP Integration->Delivery Processing->Map Item Types from ERP System to EWM

 

IMG Transaction

SPRO

Dependency

Client Dependent

Related Tables

 

 

Maintain POD Relevance

This setting ensures that when a delivery is completed, the system can automatically or manually trigger POD processes based on the item category. It acts as a control mechanism, enabling or disabling POD checks and confirmations, thereby supporting compliance, customer service, and logistics accuracy.

 

Responsible

 

SAP Module

TM

Customizing Path

IMG->Logistics Execution->Shipping->Deliveries->Proof of Delivery->Set POD-Relevance Depending on Delivery Item Category

 

IMG Transaction

SPRO

Dependency

Client Dependent

Related Tables

 

 

 

Make Shipping Point relevant for ASR

Defining a location is essential for planning and executing transportation processes. This configuration allows users to set up physical or logical locations such as plants, warehouses, customer sites, or ports. The location master includes details like geographical data, location priority, administrative information, and shipping/receiving settings. These attributes help TM determine routes, schedules, and transportation resources effectively.

 

Responsible

 

SAP Module

TM

T-Code

/N/SCMTMS/LOC3

 

IMG Transaction

NA

Dependency

Client Dependent

Related Tables

 

 

Define Loading/Unloading Point

 

Responsible

 

SAP Module

TM

T-Code

/N/SCMTMS/LOC3

IMG Transaction

NA

Dependency

Client Dependent

Related Tables

 

 

Define Staging Area and Door Determination

This configuration defines how goods are staged, and which doors are used for loading and unloading at a warehouse or distribution center. It ensures efficient coordination between transportation and warehouse operations by linking staging areas to specific doors based on criteria like warehouse number, storage type, and activity.

 

Responsible

 

SAP Module

TM

/SCWM/STADET_OUT

NA

Related Tables

 

 

 

Assign Door to Loading Point

Assign Door to Loading Point is used to links specific warehouse doors to loading points and storage control units (SCUs). It ensures efficient loading and unloading by directing goods to the correct door and bin based on warehouse layout and operational flow.

SAP Module

TM

T-Code

/N/SCWM/DOOR_SCU

IMG Transaction

NA

Dependency

Client Dependent

Related Tables

 

 

 

 

END TO END PROCESS FLOW:

 

  1. Sales Order-ECC​
  2. Outbound Delivery-ECC​
  3. Freight Unit Generation-TM​
  4. Freight Order Creation-TM​
  5. Charge calculation-TM​
  6. Subcontracting-TM ​
  7. Execution from Shipping Point-TM​
  8. Warehouse Monitor-EWM​
  9. Load or Unload Freight Order-TM​
  10. Execution at Shipping Point-TM​
  11. Execution at Customer Location-TM​
  12. Freight Settlement Document Creation-TM​

 

1. Create Sales Order

 

Role

Sales Representative

Fiori App

Create Sales Order

 

Go to the app” Create sales order”.

Enter the mandatory fields like order type and Organizational data’s like sales organization, distribution channel and division.

Enter the required details:

Sold-to-party

Ship-to-party

Material

Quantity

In the Header level, go to the shipping tab and check

Shipping Condition

Shipping Type

 

In the Item level, go to the shipping tab and check ,

Plant

Shipping Point

Route.

After checking these details, Save the document.

 

2. Create Outbound Delivery

Role

Shipping Specialist

Fiori App

Create Outbound Delivery

 

  1. Go to the app “ Create Outbound Delivery”
  2. Enter:

        Shipping Point

        Selection date

        Sales order number

        Delivery Type

  1. Enter the required Delivery Quantity and save the Document.

  • Under TM status Tab, click on the document flow under which the Freight unit is generated.

3. Create Freight Order

 

Role

Dispatcher(TM)

Fiori App

Transportation cockpit

  • Go to the app “Transportation Cockpit”.

  • Select the relevant pagelayout

  • Select the Freight unit and Select the Truck.
  • Click on “Assign Selected Items”
  • Freight Order is Created

4. Charge calculation:

  • Go the display freight order and click on edit.
  • Enter the carrier.
  • Charges will be calculated.

  • Go the display freight order and check the Charges Tab.

5. Subcontracting

  1. Click subcontracting and select “send to carrier” to send the freight order to carrier

  1. Click subcontracting and select “set to confirmed by carrier”.

6. Execution from Shipping Point

  1. Select “Execution status” and Click on “Set to ready for transportation execution” and the order will be ready for transportation execution

  • Select “Execution status” and Click on “Set in execution” and the order will be set to In-execution status.

7. Warehouse Monitor

Go to the app “Warehouse Monitor” and go to Outbound-> Outbound Delivery Order.

  • In the Outbound Delivery Order, enter the delivery number  and select “Execute”.

  • Under the Outbound Delivery Order->Follow-On Functions->Warehouse Task. Thus, the packing has been started.

  • First Create the Warehouse Request and then select “Create + Save”, warehouse order will be created.

  • Confirm the Warehouse Task, by navigating through Menu->Warehouse Task->Confirm.

  • In the Pick-HU tab, select the Create icon.
  • After the HU has been created, select the HU and click Form View.

  • In this, enter the Packaging Material and select “Create”.

  • In this, select Confirm and then Confirm + Save for the Warehouse task to be confirmed.

  • In this tab, the overall status can be viewed.

  • After the creation of HU, select “Set to Ready for Shipping”.

8. Load or Unload Freight Order

 

  • Go to the app “Load or Unload Freight Orders”.

  • Enter the Freight Order number and warehouse number.
  • Under the Warehouse Door Status, set the status to “Set to Arrived at Door”.

  • Upon Arrived, set the status to “Set Items to Loaded”.

  • After the Items are loaded, set the status to “Set Stop to Loading Completed”.

  • After Loading has been completed, set the status to “Set to Departed from Door”.

  • Goods Posting will be done automatically.

9. Execution at Shipping Point

  • Now, under the Execution Status tab set status to “Set to Arrived”.
  • Upon arrival, set the status to “Set to Checked In”.

  • Now set the status to “Set to Checked Out”.

  • Upon Checked out, set the status to “Set to Departed”.

10. Execution at Customer Location

  • Now, under the Execution Status tab set status to “Set to Arrived”.

  • Upon arrival, set the status to “Set to Checked In”.

  • Upon Checked In, set the status to “Set to Unloaded”.

  • After Unloaded, set the status to “Set to Checked Out”.

  • Under the  Statuses tab ,you can see the Goods Movement status as “Completely Processed”.

11. Freight Settlement Document Creation

  • Go to charge/Settlement, select Create Freight Settlement Document.

  • Check document consistency and click on “Post” and the freight settlement document gets created.

 

CONCLUSION

ASR brings transportation and warehouse execution together under one unified Freight Order framework. This reduces complexity, improves synchronization, and supports dynamic planning scenarios. In modern supply chains, ASR serves as a strategic enabler for efficient and transparent logistics operations.

I hope this blog helps you better understand and implement ASR in your TM–EWM landscape. Feel free to share your thoughts or questions in the comments.

__JEYADURGA MANIVEL__

entry.title: Re: Reading GOS(Generic Object Services) in MM03
entry.content:
Hi @rmz01, you need to create a custom CDS entity along with a class implementation, as shown in the code from the earlier post, to read the GOS attachments. You must read the material number or sales order number (as applicable in your case) and then implement the same logic to fetch and display the GOS attachments on the frontend. At the end of this implementation, you will receive all GOS attachments in XSTRING format, and you can convert them to Base64 if required. You can also maintain a custom table to track all the attachments that were uploaded or deleted.  

do look into my profile i have posted about other scenarios to delete and create gos : About SanketGode45 - SAP Community

entry.title: Re: Why ABAP do not have JSON de/serializer in kernel?
entry.content:
@alexey_arseniev I understand all points related to wide functionality around types in ABAP.
But I guess it shifts the focus. My view on such standard serializer, that it should support JSON in ABAP, and not full ABAP in JSON.
E.g. choose some basic types(wider versions in case of similar types) in ABAP which will be associated with JSON types and define that only they are supported (JSON boolean and null can be a challenge but should be solvable).

The other staff related to value conversions (like conversion exits, date/times/timestamps, different table types, different numericals and etc), I guess is more close to application logic itself, and so can be ignored.

Of course different naming rules and name mapping(mainly to support length limit) will be asked, and it is nice to have, but can be a second priority.
 
Thank you for the hint, I will try to make the request clearer.

P.S. thank you for the work on /ui2/cl_json, it is used in many projects I worked on (by the way in majority cases just with camelCase namings option) . Also I learned from you that macros could be a way to make something faster in ABAP 🙂

entry.title: Automated Invoice and Credit Memo Processing Using ERS in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud
entry.content:
Automated Invoice and Credit Memo Processing Using ERS in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud

Introduction

In a standard procurement process, materials are procured from a supplier through purchase order creation, goods receipt posting, invoice processing, and vendor payment. When Evaluated Receipt Settlement (ERS) is activated, the system automatically creates the supplier invoice based on the goods receipt, eliminating the need for manual invoice posting.

During one of the SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud implementations, a requirement came up to handle supplier return scenarios using ERS. In cases where materials were found defective after receipt and payment, the business expected the system to automatically generate a credit memo once the returned goods were posted. This blog explains the end-to-end process that was configured and tested in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud to achieve this automation.

Step 1: Maintain Business Partner Master Data

 Use the Manage Business Partner Master Data app.

Ensure the following fields are enabled for the Supplier in the Purchasing tab:

  • Automatic Evaluated Receipt Settlement
  • Evaluated Receipt Settlement
  • Goods Receipt-Based Invoice Verification

These settings allow the system to generate invoices and credit memos automatically without manual invoice posting.

Step 1: Image 1

Step 2: Schedule ERS Background Job 

Use the app: Schedule Supplier Invoice Jobs – Advanced

Step 2: Image 1

Select the job template: Evaluated Receipt Settlement

This job automatically:

  • Creates Supplier Invoices for GR
  • Creates Credit Memos for Returns

I have created scheduled background jobs for ERS using this app.

Step 2: Image 2

 

Step 2: Image 3

Step 2: Image 4

Step 2: Image 5

Step 3: Create Standard Purchase Order (ERS Enabled)

Create a standard PO.

Ensure:

 ERS checkbox is automatically enabled in the Invoice tab.

Step 3: Image 1

Post Goods Receipt (GRN).

Once the scheduled ERS job runs: System automatically generates the Supplier Invoice document. The below image shows the PO history reflecting the automatically created invoice document.

Step 3: Image 2

In the Schedule Supplier Invoice Jobs – Advanced app, the below image shows the scheduled job that automatically generated the supplier invoice based on the ERS configuration.

Step 3: Image 3

Step 3: Image 4

Note: In SAP S/4HANA Cloud (Public Edition), the classic GUI transaction MRRL used for Evaluated Receipt Settlement is not available. Instead, ERS processing is executed through the Schedule Supplier Invoice Jobs - Advanced app.

Step 4: Vendor Payment

Based on the automatically generated invoice:

  • Vendor payment is processed and cleared using standard payment process.

Return Process Using ERS

Step 5: Create Return Purchase Order

When defective material needs to be returned:

  • Create PO with Return Indicator
  • Ensure ERS is enabled in the PO

Step 5: Image 1

Step 6: Post Return Goods Receipt

Post Goods Receipt for the Return PO.

Once the scheduled ERS job runs:

  • System automatically generates a Credit Memo
  • No manual invoice posting required
  • Liability is adjusted automatically

Step 6: Image 1

In the Schedule Supplier Invoice Jobs – Advanced app, the below image shows the scheduled job that automatically generated the credit memo for the supplier return process based on the ERS configuration.

Step 6: Image 2

Step 6: Image 3

Conclusion

In my experience, ERS in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud works smoothly not only for regular procurement but also for supplier return scenarios, provided the configuration is done correctly. Once the supplier master is properly maintained and the background job is scheduled, the system automatically takes care of invoice generation for goods receipts and credit memo creation for returns.

The key is to ensure that ERS is consistently enabled in both the standard and return purchase orders. When everything is aligned, the process runs without manual intervention, reducing effort for the finance team and avoiding posting errors. This makes the overall P2P cycle more controlled and efficient.

entry.title: Need Guidance: MRP-Area Based Scheduling for Subcontracting Components (REM + Stock Determination)
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

We have a requirement to generate different schedule lines for the same component based on the parent material’s PIR source (i.e., FG/parent material). The scenario spans buy materials, subcontracting materials, and components in subcontracting BOMs.

Requirement

When a component is common across multiple FGs, its source of supply should be selected based on the originating FG:

  • Example: Component COMP1 exists in FG1 and FG2.
    • If the requirement originates from FG1, MRP should select SCH1 (Scheduling Agreement 1).
    • If it originates from FG2, MRP should select SCH2 (Scheduling Agreement 2).

Context: We are in a REM scenario. Components are consumed via stock determination rule/group; after MRP, created planned orders are consumed upon MFBF posting.

Approach Adopted

To drive different schedule lines for the same component, we proposed:

  • MRP execution by MRP Area (MRP Area = Storage Location)
  • Maintain two different scheduling agreements in the source list, separated by MRP Area.

Result:
When we maintain the storage location in the BOM, the requirement flows to the respective MRP Area, and we can generate different schedule lines for the same component. This works fine for:

  • Normal buy materials, and
  • Subcontracting materials (header)

Issues Faced

1) Subcontracting Component Requirements Ignoring MRP Areas

For components of a subcontracting material, the requirement is always pushed to the plant level, even when MRP Areas are maintained. Consequently, only one schedule line is picked from the source list.

  • Tried BADIs MD_EXT_SUP and MD_MODIFY_SOURCE, but they don’t help because the BAdIs receive the aggregated requirement without a clear reference to the originating FG/requirement source.

Ask: Guidance or pointers on how to ensure subcontracting components respect MRP Area–based sourcing so that MRP picks different schedule lines based on the originating FG.

2) Stock Determination Disrupted by Storage Location in BOM

Maintaining the storage location in the BOM (for COMP1) disrupts our stock determination rule—stock is then consumed only from the BOM’s fixed storage location (standard SAP behavior).

Mitigation Attempt: Two Production Versions

  • PV1 / BOM1: For MFBF posting (no storage location in BOM) → stock determination works correctly.
  • PV2 / BOM2: Identical to BOM1 but with storage location maintained for MRP Area–based MRP → ensures different schedule lines during MRP.

Problem:
MRP creates planned orders with PV2, but MFBF postings are executed with PV1. As a result, planned orders are not consumed and remain open.

Ask: How should we proceed?

  • Option 1 (Single BOM):
    • Pros: MRP creates different schedule lines; planned orders are consumed during MFBF
    • Con: Stock determination rule is disrupted
  • Option 2 (Two BOMs / PVs):
    • Pros: MFBF posting is correct; different schedule lines created correctly
    • Con: Planned orders remain unconsumed

Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: Discrepancy: PAL consumption quantity is 0 despite co...
entry.content:
Hello kazuya-nakamura46,

this sounds like you found a bug in the system, as there are no known cases in Batch BOP where the system updates the confirmed quantity in the business document but skips the update to PAL consumption buckets.

Technically there should be no difference how interactive BOP and batch BOP handles PAL consumptions. In both cases the consumptions should be in sync with the final confirmations in the order. Otherwise you would have an inconsistency between consumption and order confirmation, which should not happen.

I recommend to open a support ticket, so that SAP experts can investigate this specific case directly in your system.

Best regards,
Carsten

entry.title: Mastering Purchase Order Accruals in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
The SAP S/4HANA Accrual Engine, Accruals Accounting offers flexible configuration options like,

  • Calculation and posting of the accruals
  • Option to review process for the monthly accrual amounts
  • Supports monitoring and the approval of accruals

POAC Introduction

The Purchase Order Accruals application enables you to calculate and post accruals in General Ledger Accounting automatically.

The relevant data is transferred from the Materials Management component to the accrual engine and automatically converted from purchase order items into accrual items. The system calculates the amount to accrue for each purchase order item based on the above data. You can also simulate these amounts.

The functions in Purchase Order Accruals support parallel Accounting. This means that you can calculate and post accruals simultaneously according to different ledger groups. You can start an accrual run, which posts all accruals for the various business transactions.

From a technical standpoint, the Purchase Order Accruals application is built on the accrual engine. The purchase order items to be accrued are managed in Purchase Order Accruals. The processes for calculating and posting accruals, on the other hand, are managed in the Accrual Engine.

To post-purchase order accruals, perform the following steps:

  • In the MM component, create a purchase order
  • The purchase order is then transferred to the Accrual Engine offline or online, and monthly accrual amounts are calculated and displayed in the Accrual Engine
  • (Optional) Review and approve, or just approve the monthly accrual amounts
  • Post the accrual amounts.


Accruals Process
The purchase order accruals process involves the following job templates and apps in Fiori:

  • The Propose Periodic Accruals job template in the Schedule Accruals Jobs app
  • Review Purchase Order Accruals - My Inbox or Review Purchase Order Accruals - for Cost Accountant
  • The Post Periodic Accruals job template in the Schedule Accruals Jobs app
  • Analyse Accruals Posting
  • Display Purchase Order Accruals

Integration
In Financial Accounting, you can also post accruals with the recurring entries function. In contrast to recurring entries, the functions in Purchase Order Accruals offer you the following benefits:

  • Automatic calculation based on live PO data.
  • Pre-posting review/approval.
  • Complex rules (e.g., pro-rata, thresholds).

Purchase Order Accruals: Design

 

Purchase Order Accruals Design

POAC Steps
Creation of a purchase order in application component Purchasing MM-PUR, for example using transaction “Create Purchase Order” (ME21N).

Each purchase order is automatically transferred into the Accrual Engine. This happens by Customizing settings and controlling whether and how accruals will be posted. In the Accrual Engine, an accrual object is created for each purchase order.

While implementing the application component Purchase Order Accruals, you have to transfer your existing purchase orders once into the Accrual Engine of your production system. You can do these using accrual activities “Transfer Purchase Orders to Accrual Engine” with schedule accruals jobs app ID F3778.

Display Accrual Objects:
For each purchase order, you can display the monthly accrual amounts the system calculates using app “Display Accrual Objects items” app ID F4899.

Schedule Accruals Jobs:
This app enables you to schedule accruals jobs using the templates provided. For example, you can perform periodic posting runs and propose periodic accrual amounts. 

You can use the scheduling options to carry out the following periodic accrual activities:

  • Perform Periodic Posting Runs
  • Propose Period Amounts
  • Transfer Purchase Orders to Accrual Engine

The templates are available only when you've registered them in customizing view APJ_C_SCOPE . Follow the steps to register for the three templates:

  1. Run t-code SM30.
  2. Enter APJ_C_SCOPE for Table/View, and click Maintain.
  3. Add the following new entries:
    • SAP_FIN_GL_ACE_POSTING_RUN
    • SAP_FIN_GL_ACE_PROPOSAL_RUN
    • SAP_FIN_GL_POAC_MMPO2ACE_TRANS
  4. Save your changes.

 

Schedule Accruals Jobs

 

Template Selection

Optional: Review and approval process at the end of each fiscal period:

  • At the end of each fiscal period, a reviewer can adjust the accrual amounts (or planned costs) that the system proposes for the current fiscal period.
  • As a prerequisite, the proposal amounts must be generated for the given fiscal period using Accrual Engine for POs - Propose Period Amounts under schedule accruals jobs.
  • The review can be performed using the app “Review Purchase Order Accruals for Cost Accountant” app ID F3552. The review includes the possibility to manually adjust the accrual amounts that the system proposes.
  • As an alternative or in addition to the review, approval can be performed using the transaction “Approve Periodic Accruals”.
  • The difference between review and approval is that in case only review is active, then the system will post the proposed accrual amounts even if no review was done. But if approval is active, then only those accrual amounts are posted which have been approved. 

Post accruals at the end of each fiscal period: 

The accrual amounts are posted using accrual activities “Periodic Posting Run” with schedule accruals jobs app ID F3778.

Analyze Accrual Postings: 

With this application you can display an overview of accrual postings, you can filter the postings by various criteria and navigate to a specific posting in related apps to investigate. (App ID F3732)

Display Accrual Objects

  • An accrual object contains all data that the system needs to calculate and post accruals for this business transaction.
  • Optional: Reviewing and Approving Periodic Accruals
  • You can set up a review and approval procedure for the accrual amounts calculated and proposed by the system.

Customizing Purchase Order Accruals

  • Before you can use the functions in Purchase Order Accruals, you must make extensive Customizing settings.

  • The settings control, for example, the purchase orders or purchase order items to be accrued, the algorithms to be used for a particular purchase order item, and the accounts to be used for accruals posting.
  • If you want to take the optional step of manually checking and approving accrual amounts, you must also make the corresponding Customizing settings here.
  • Additionally, some settings are available to you with which you can adjust the functions according to your needs. For example, you can add user-defined fields (or parameters) for use in the functions. 

Posting Data of an Accrual Object

For each accrual object, the item data is saved in the journal.

Prerequisites:

You have made the necessary settings in Customizing for accrual posting, that is, you have, for example, defined accrual item categories, posting schemas, and account determination. You find these settings in Customizing for Accrual Management.

Closing Notes:

Details of the Purchase order Accruals process can be obtained under Scope item 2VB in SAP Best Practices Explorer. POAC streamlines month-end but PO lifecycle changes (e.g., changes, cancellations) impact accruals—test thoroughly in production-like scenarios.

Thanks for reading! Feedback welcome to refine future posts.

entry.title: Re: Issue with “Stage Material for Production Order” App...
entry.content:
Good day @kkumara 

Thank you for your question.

Based on your screenshots, the following is visible:

  1. Your Selection Horizon for Requirements dated does not include the order component requirement date. I can see your requirement date is set to 02.13. 2026 but your Selection Period for requirements is set to 02.26.2026. This means your new order’s component requirements are outside the horizon you selected and therefore no line items appear. I suggest you set the selection horizon to a date range that covers the component requirement date, then execute once more. See the following reference for more details: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blog-posts-by-sap/production-integration-with-warehouse-management-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud-demo/ba-p/13553882
  2. Even if the order is released, the order will not appear if the components are not considered as staging relevant for the selected staging type (for example EWM Rel. Parts) or if key master data is missing. Basically saying the app only shows orders where staging is relevant and possible. See Material Staging (with EWM) for more insights.

The reason you do not get a message when you click stage is, the Stage button only triggers something if at least one replenishment element line exists in the lower “Replen. Elements” table. If the list is empty, pressing Stage will not create anything, you will see no “Saved” or “Delivery created” message.

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: INTELLIGENT INVOICE PROCESSING
entry.content:
From Text-Only to Multi-modal: How Vision, Audio and Structured Data Are Changing AI in Business

Multi-model AI refers a system platform where it will process, manage and understand multiple types of data which includes not only the text, but also images, video, audio and structured data (tables). Multi-model AI provides efficient representations where the output will be error prone and more accurate.

Main usage of multi-model AI in SAP:

  • Automation of end-to-end process.
  • Increases the analyst productivity.
  • Reduces the manual work.
  • Accurate in fetching data.

Issue: Manually entering the data in the invoices (Table, PDF, images) and the manual GL account and cost center assignment

Account Payable Bottlenecks:

High labour cost: Manual entering of data and its validation consumes more manpower.

Error: While entering manual data, chances for error including incorrect GL account, mismatch tax code etc.

On-boarding: The process of on-boarding new suppliers is very critical as it includes collecting respective documents.

Inconsistent validation: Matching purchase order and good receipt against invoices are manual and very slow.

Delayed approvals and payments: Due to the invalid data entered in the invoices the approvals are getting rejected and the payment flow is disturbed.

The above issues will lead to duplicate invoices, delayed payments and approvals, incorrect report values.

 

Solution: The multi-model solution - SAP AI & Machine learning

How multimodal AI works?

  1. Ingestion
  2. OCR & recognizing the data
  3. Extraction using ML
  4. Upload invoices
  5. Output of the process.

 

Multimodal AI: Intelligent Invoice Processing in SAP Business Network

Intelligent Invoice Processing refers to the automation of invoice to pay cycle with the help of machine learning, AI where this implemented in the SAP platform named SAP Business Network.

The technology uses numerous amounts of data extraction, learning the data, validation checking and workflow structures for the efficient output.

Technology involved:

 

SAP Document Information Extraction (DOX): This service uses Optical Character Recognition (OCR), Natural Language Processing (NCP) and machine learning to “read” the unstructured data. It automatically extracts and separates header data (document date, document type, invoice number, amount) and line-item data to reduce the manual scanning.

It also provides one document interface where the users can develop and review the rules for extracting the invoice data.

 

Business rules and Workflow Engine:

SAP Business Network applies defined rules as it maintains tolerance limits for posting the invoice, it will check for tax codes, also for approval hierarchies for accelerating the accounts payable invoice life cycle.

SAP APP: SAP Business Network

  1. In this app the unstructured data (PDF) must be uploaded in the upload invoice file inside Business Network to import bulk of invoices.
  2. The feature automatically converts the invoices from PDF format to electronic format.
  3. It automatically maps the respective fields in the electronic invoice using annotation rules and machine learning.
  4. Suppliers can implement their own templates for the custom data to meet business requirements.
  5. The invoices send through the mails can be retrieved automatically, extracts and then invoices will be created.
  6. Exceptions are handled as if any data missed in the uploaded input, it will be handled by exception management.

SAP Business Network tools:

1. Intelligent invoice Conversion

What it does:

This tool will allow suppliers to upload image invoices, upload pdfs into the SAP business network which will be later converted into electronic invoices using AI.

Key features:

Self-configuration setup: Suppliers will configure their own templates without the help of IT assistance.

Map by AI:

The application will automatically extract the data which includes header and line item data and maps to the respective XML format.

Park as Draft:

 The converted invoices will be available in a Draft format to review for the verification before submission.

Availability: This tool is applicable for enterprise suppliers in the SAP Business Network.

2. PDF invoicing

What it does:

This will allow suppliers to send pdf invoices in a mail to a maintained address.

The SAP Business Network retrieves these mails, extracts and creates an invoice.

Key features:

Automatic Conversion: Here also PDF invoices will be converted into XML format, and it will be processed further.

Access: Available for both Standard and enterprise suppliers.

3. Commerce Automation

What it does:

For the large mass of suppliers, the invoices will be send through the integration between ERP system and the SAP business network.

Method:

Same as the above tools the invoice will be formatted in the XML format.

Output:

This tool is the most automated, without manual intervention the invoices will be uploaded directly.

4. Automatic Invoice creation from Receipts

What it does:

It allows evaluated receipt settlement (ERS), so that the buyer will generate a invoice based on the posted goods receipt.

Key features:

No need for supplier in this case.

Prevents the invoice rejections as the invoice will be matched with the goods receipt automatically.

Supports: PO based invoices.

 

5. CSV upload

What it does:

This tool will allow the suppliers to upload a CSV file which contains invoice to the SAP business Network where the invoices will be posted automatically.

Supports:

This is mainly used by medium volume suppliers where the EDI is impossible but having lot of invoices for processing.

SAP Business Network     Source: community.sap.com

Benefits:

  1. Efficiency is improved by prioritizing tasks, repetitive work is automated.
  2. Instant KPI’s with lower finance costs, accurate reports.
  3. Converting complex data into insightful simpler data.
  4. Improve Operational efficiency.
  5. Supports various file formats includes PDF, PNG, JPEG, TIFF.

Conclusion:

From the above blog, processing invoices are very much easy procedure which involves SAP business Network where it uses AI technology to prevent manual errors, delayed payments, to increase the process efficiency. The buyers and the suppliers will have a seamless collaboration.

And also, AI used in wide areas to reduce costs, greater compliance where it leads to digital transformation.

For your reference:

Use case: https://www.sap.com/use-cases/reduce-time-spent-summarizing-financial-data

Use case: https://www.sap.com/assetdetail/2023/10/7aeaa7e2-917e-0010-bca6-c68f7e60039b.html

entry.title: S/4HANA Cloud: How to Retrieve Print Queue PDFs via OData API
entry.content:
S/4HANA Cloud: How to Retrieve Print Queue PDFs via API

Introduction

When building side-by-side extensions or integrating external applications with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, a common requirement is to retrieve generated PDF documents (like Invoices or Purchase Orders) programmatically.SAP provides a dedicated standard pattern: The Print Queue API (SAP_COM_0466).
Overview | Print Queue Item - Read (A2X) | SAP Business Accelerator Hub

Step 1: Communication Setup

First, you need to expose the API through a Communication Arrangement.

  1. Create a Communication User: Set up a technical user and password in the Maintain Communication Users app.

     

    ex. SHU_TEST_UESR

     

  2. Activate the Arrangement: Create a new arrangement for scenario SAP_COM_0466 (Printing - Pull Integration).

     


    Important: Ensure the arrangement is saved and its status is Active. API permissions will not trigger if it remains in "Draft" mode.

Step 2: Print Queue Configuration (The Secret Sauce)

This is where most developers get stuck. Even with an active API, the service will return empty results unless the Print Queue is explicitly configured to allow your technical user.

  1. Open the Maintain Print Queues app.
  2. Create a New Queue: Do not use the DEFAULT queue, as it often has restricted permissions. Create a custom one (e.g., Z_PULL_TEST).
  3. Assign the User: Inside the queue settings, navigate to the Assigned Users section and add your Communication User.
Without this assignment, the API will return a 200 OK but with zero data, as the user doesn't have "permission to see" the contents of that specific vault.

Step 3: Retrieving the PDF via API

The service API_CLOUD_PRINT_PULL_SRV uses Function Imports. You must follow a specific sequence of calls using Basic Authentication.

1. Verify Connectivity

Check which queues your user can access:

GET
.../sap/opu/odata/sap/API_CLOUD_PRINT_PULL_SRV/Get_PrintQueuesOfUser

2. Fetch the Item ID

Retrieve the ID for the next available document in the queue:

GET
.../RetrieveNextQueueItem?Qname='Z_EXTERNAL_PULL'&Language='EN'

Copy the item_id from the XML response (e.g., FA163ECE...).

 

3. Download the PDF Content

Use the Item ID to pull the Base64 encoded binary data:

GET
.../Get_QItem_Document?attachments=false&main_doc=true&item_id='YOUR_ITEM_ID'&qname='Z_EXTERNAL_PULL'

Step 4: Handling the Result

The response will contain a <d:Blob> tag. This is a Base64 encoded string of the PDF. To view the content, you simply need to decode this string in your application logic (or use a tool like Postman’s Visualizer).

 

<d:Blob>JVBERi0xLj... (Long Base64 String)</d:Blob>
Pro Tip: Once downloaded, it is best practice to call the Set_QItem_Status function to mark the document as "Printed" or "Successful" so it is removed from the pending pull list.

Conclusion

By shifting from the internal Output Management service to the Print Queue Pull API (SAP_COM_0466), you can reliably and securely automate document retrieval from S/4HANA Cloud. The key takeaways are ensuring the Active status of your Communication Arrangement and the explicit Assignment of the User to the custom Print Queue.

Happy coding!

entry.title: Error during Portugal Settlement Document Creation – Series Not Registered for Validation Code
entry.content:
System: SAP S/4HANA (Development)

Country: Portugal (PT)

Issue Description:
During the creation of settlement documents for Portugal with digital signature enabled, the system is preventing document posting with an error related to series validation.

Error Message:

Series LPXX is not registered for validation code; posting is not allowed.

Configuration Performed:
The following configurations have been completed:

  1. Number range maintained for Portugal using object GLO_PT_SET.
  2. Number ranges recorded for the digital signature of settlement documents.
  3. The same number range maintained under object W_LFAKTURA.
  4. Country/Region-specific number ranges defined by assigning the same internal number range to country PT.

Observed Behavior:
Despite completing the above configurations, the system throws the validation code error while creating the settlement document and does not allow posting.

Testing Status:
The issue is currently being tested in the development system. Once the issue is resolved and confirmed, the business will proceed with registering the series on the Portuguese Tax Authority portal.

Request:
Kindly advise if any additional configuration or prerequisite is required to resolve this error, or confirm whether series registration and validation code maintenance is mandatory even for development/testing environments.

Thanks and Regards,

Pankaj

entry.title: What’s New in Subscription Order Management 2025 FPS01
entry.content:
What’s New in Subscription Order Management 2025 FPS01 

New Release 

PUBLIC 

 

 

 February 2026 

 

🚀 Introducing Subscription Order Management 2025 FPS01 

Advancing Agility, Intelligence, and Customer Experience Across the Subscription Lifecycle 

We are excited to announce the 2025 FPS01 release for Subscription Order Management (SOM)—a significant upgrade that strengthens end-to-end subscription lifecycle processes, enhances user experience, and unlocks new flexibility for solution extensibility. 

This release builds on the foundations of SAP BRIM in S/4HANA, expanding support for recurring services, improving Fiori usability, enabling phased contract management in the UI, and extending key API capabilities. Whether you manage complex subscription bundles, long-term phased contracts, consumption-based products, or recurring physical deliveries—this release elevates how business users and developers interact with SOM. 

 

⭐ Key Innovations in 2025 FPS01 

Recurring Delivery Items Now Available in Fiori Apps 

Originally introduced in 2025 FPS0, recurring delivery of physical products is now integrated directly into the Subscription Contract Lifecycle Management Fiori apps. Business users can now: 

  • Access delivery plan items via Fiori navigation 
  • Review detailed delivery schedules (delivery plan lines) within contract item views 
  • Manage physical product deliveries alongside digital or service-based subscriptions 

This brings a consistent and modern UI for managing hybrid subscription models. 

Business Benefits 

  • Unified workspace for hybrid offerings: Bring subscriptions with digital services and recurring physical delivery products together in a single Fiori experience. 
  • Reduced operational complexity: Reduced jumping between SAP GUI and Fiori for delivery management. 
  • Better order accuracy and transparency: Delivery plan details are always available where subscription decisions are made. 

For more information, refer to Fiori Enablement of Subscriptions with Recurring Delivery of Physical Products. 

 

Phased Contracts — Now Manageable Directly in the UI 

Previously supported only via API, lifecycle management of phased subscription contracts can now be fully handled in the UI. 

New capabilities include: 

  • “Add Successor Phase” button at item level on the subscription order user interface 
  • Automatic delimitation of previous phases 
  • Ability to modify attributes (pricing, quantity, durations) per phase 
  • Clear visibility of current vs. future phases in the subscription contract 
  • Implementation of contract changes using standard and customized change processes 

This enables business teams to easily model: 

  • Multi-year upgrades 
  • Scheduled migrations 
  • Stepwise expansions or reductions in services 
  • Timesliced price adjustments

 

 

 

 

Business Benefits 

  • Empowers business agility: Sales and operations teams can model multi-phase deals (e.g., gradual upgrades or scheduled transitions) without IT intervention. 
  • More accurate revenue planning: Contract phases reflect real commercial timing—improving forecasting and billing accuracy. 
  • Reduced implementation cost: Eliminates the need for custom UI extensions or external tooling to manage phase transitions. 

For more information, refer to UI Enablement of Phased Contracts . 

 Fiori UX Enhancements for Subscription Lifecycle Management 

FPS01 brings a redesigned user experience to the Manage Subscription Contract Lifecycle Fiori app. 

What’s new: 

  • Contract State Hierarchy View 
    See contract structure across current, planned, and expired states in a single intuitive hierarchy. 
  • Harmonized Navigation Between WebUI and Fiori 
    All subscription-related navigations now default to Fiori apps and navigation to WebUI is integrated into a single menu, offering seamless transitions and reduced UI fragmentation.  
  • Improved Timeslice Navigation 
    At the main item level, user navigation is updated for cleaner contract timelines. 

These refinements simplify review of complex contracts—especially multi-level bundles and contracts with phases. 

Business Benefits 

  • Faster decision making: Clear visibility into contract phases (planned, current, expired) helps business users understand the contract lifecycle status instantly. 
  • Lower training and support cost: Unified WebUI → Fiori navigation reduces confusion and simplifies onboarding. 
  • Improved productivity: Timeslice-based navigation avoids mis-clicks and ensures users land on relevant contract elements. 

 

Maintain Plant and Storage Locations in Subscription Orders 

For organizations delivering physical goods alongside service subscriptions, FPS01 introduces plant and storage location fields for: 

  • Subscription items 
  • Sales items 
  • Master agreements 

Highlights: 

  • Existing plant determination logic is respected 
  • Plant / storage locations flow to subscription contracts and SD sales orders 
  • Supports scenarios with multiple fulfilment centres 

This greatly improves operational accuracy for hybrid subscription models. 

 

 Business Benefits 

  • Operational accuracy for hybrid models: Ensures the correct fulfilment location is assigned for physical components of subscription offerings. 
  • Improved logistics coordination: Enables tighter alignment between subscription sales and downstream supply-chain processes. 
  • Minimized errors in follow-on documents: Correct plant/storage values automatically flow into subscription contracts and SD sales orders. 

For more information, refer to View and Update the Plant and Storage Location Values While Creating a Subscription Order . 

 

Other Enhancements 

➡️Register Payment Cards Directly in Subscription Transactions 

A major usability upgrade: Payment cards can now be registered directly on subscription orders or solution quotations. 

Key improvements: 

  • No prerequisite to register the card under the business partner 
  • Card token (from Digital Payments Hub) is stored in the transaction 
  • Token is passed automatically to the subscription contract and provider contract 

This removes friction in subscription sales scenarios and accelerates order capture. 

Business Benefits 

  • Faster checkout and quote-to-contract cycles: Direct card registration removes bottlenecks during order and quotation capture. 
  • Reduced fallout in sales workflows: No need to navigate to business partner master data to register payment details. 
  • Enhanced customer experience: Sales teams and self-service portals can collect payment details more seamlessly. 

For more information, refer to Registering Payment Cards at Subscription Transaction Level . 

 

➡️ Manage Sub-Items During Contract Extension via API 

The Change Quotation API now allows the following actions on product bundles: 

  • Adding new sub-items 
  • Deleting existing sub-items (with constraints) 
  • Extending selected sub-items during contract renewals 

This provides developers a fine-grained control mechanism to adjust complex subscription bundles during contract extension cycles. 

Business Benefits 

  • Greater flexibility in contract adjustments: Supports real-world scenarios such as product bundle expansions or optional service removals. 
  • Improved automation for renewals: System-managed sub-item logic eliminates manual adjustments during extension cycles. 
  • Stronger governance: Mandatory bundle rules are enforced, ensuring compliance with product design. 

For more information, refer to Enhancements to the Solution Quotation (A2X) OData API . 

 

➡️Recurring Sales Items with Delivery Plans — Now Supported in APIs 

The OData APIs API_SUBSCRIPTIONORDER and API_SUBSCRIPTIONCONTRACT now support: 

  • Creation of subscription orders that include recurring sales items with delivery plans 
  • Reading subscription contracts containing recurring sales items 
  • Delivery plan data via 1:n associations 

This enables automation and integration for businesses with periodic physical deliveries tied to subscriptions. 

Business Benefits 

  • Better integration with external systems: Partners and customers can automate creation of subscription orders with periodic deliveries. 
  • Consistent multi-channel orchestration: API parity ensures front-office systems (CPQ, commerce, custom portals) behave exactly like SAP SOM. 
  • Insightful downstream reporting: Delivery plan data is available programmatically for planning, fulfilment, and analytics. 

For more information, refer to Enhancements to the Subscription Order (Version 0002) OData API and Enhancements to the Subscription Contract - Read OData API  . 

 

➡️Best Practice Content Enhancements (Scope Item 7WB) 

The 7WB - Subscription Order Management – Master Data and Basic Functions scope item has been enhanced so that you can now execute change processes on an existing subscription contract and implement the following.  

  • Enable trigger of change process on an existing contract supporting relevant change processes 
  • Submit the change order to update the subscription contract 
  • Replicate these changes to FICA provider contract 
  • Enable replication of the changes to FICA provider contract, via the order distribution interface 
  • Leverage the subscription contract lifecycle app to display the new subscription contract 

The enhancement builds on the previous capability of creating new subscription contracts and accelerates customer onboarding with ready-to-deploy business scenarios.  

Business Benefits 

  • Accelerated project timelines: Customers can adopt Subscription Order Management with lower setup complexity. 
  • Reduced implementation risk: Standardized flows minimize the likelihood of design issues during rollout. 
  • Improved cross-team alignment: Consistent best practice processes help cross-functional teams work more cohesively. 

For more information, refer to Subscription Order Management – Master Data and Basic Functions (7WB) . 

🔍 Additional Resources 

  • SAP BRIM Product Page  
  • Product Assistance – Subscription Order Management 
  • SAP Business Accelerator Hub (API Documentation)  
  • BRIM Community  

Subscription Order Management 2025 FPS00 has been released to customers on October 8th, 2025. For more information about Subscription Order Management 2025 FPS00, check out the following links: 

  • What’s New in Subscription Order Management 2025 FPS01 (Go to Finance > Billing and Revenue Innovation Management > Subscription Order Management) 
  • Product Assistance on the SAP Help Portal 
  • Integration Guide: Integration Guide for S/4HANA BRIM using SOM 
  • Business AI in BRIM:  
  • Joule Help Page      
  • Initial Setup of Joule for BRIM 
  • Questions and Answers for Subscription Order Management in SAP Community 
  • SAP BRIM Customer Influence Portal > Opportunity area “Solution Quotation and BRIM” 

 

💬 Final Thoughts 

The Subscription Order Management 2025 FPS01 release represents a major step forward for enterprises managing complex subscription offerings. With improved UX, enriched APIs, expanded delivery plan support, and powerful phased contract capabilities, this release empowers organizations to scale subscriptions with greater agility, control, and customer focus.

entry.title: Re: Error during Portugal Settlement Document Creation –...
entry.content:
The root cause is under "Testing Status" of your post.

The error “Series LPXX is not registered for validation code; posting is not allowed” is triggered because the series has not yet been registered with the Portuguese Tax Authority and therefore no validation code exists in the system.

Once digital signature is activated in SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Portugal, the system enforces the legal requirement that every series used for billing/settlement must be registered with AT and have a valid validation code. Without that registration, the system will block postings — even in a development/test environment. So the missing registration/validation code is indeed the reason for the error.

entry.title: Re: Transport Request Error Object TVDIR,TDDAT cannot be locked
entry.content:
It's not very clear what means "While locking object in transport request". What did you do exactly? SE10 > Menu Transport Request > Object List > Lock? On the task or on the transport request?

I guess it's the message TK022. It's sent by the function module TRINT_LOCK_OBJECT.

NB: concerned messages in plain text for easier Web text indexing:

  • Object R3TR TABU TDDAT cannot be locked
  • Object R3TR TABU TVDIR cannot be locked

TDDAT and TVDIR concern the Table Maintenance Dialogs (generated by TMG).

entry.title: Workflow for Sales quotations and Sales Orders triggered when the end user changes the Item Price
entry.content:
This requirement is to be implemented in the SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud system.

To trigger a workflow in the Sales Quotation and Sales Order applications, a condition must be checked for the item price. The workflow should be triggered only if the item price has been changed by the end user. To achieve this, a sales quotation or sales order simulation needs to be executed to determine the system-calculated item price. This value must then be compared with the price entered or modified by the user. If a difference is identified, the workflow should be triggered accordingly.

The main challenge is retrieving the item price within the custom logic. However, this can be addressed by calling the Sales Order Simulation API, which provides the calculated item price for comparison.

Pre-requisties : 

  • Required Business Roles must be assigned to the user. 
  • Workflows for Sales Orders must be configured and activated in the system. Refer SAP Help on flexible workflow setup.  https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/a376cd9ea00d476b96f18dea1247e6a5/5a5f3f686c06490893a588c99b36adca.html

Solution: 

When sales quotation and sales order created/changed in S/4 system then the Business Add-In (BAdI) SD_APM_SET_APPROVAL_REASON is provided to specify whether a sales document is relevant for approvals or not. Refer below documentation for this BADI.  https://api.sap.com/badi/SD_APM_SET_APPROVAL_REASON

In this Blog, Sales order is used for reference to explain the use case. 

Communication Setup:

  1. In order to execute API for simulation of the creation of sales order inside Cloud Badi implementation (SD_APM_SET_APPROVAL_REASON),  required communcation arrangement to be setup for the Communication Scenario SAP_COM_0109 (Sales Order Integration) to technically enable and activate the Sales Order simulation API - 'API_SALESORDERSIMULATION'.  Refer API Hub for detail :  https://api.sap.com/api/sap-s4-CE_SALESORDERSIMULATION_0001-v1/overview
  2. Refer SAP Help for guidance on setting up the communication arrangement, system and communication user.   https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/2e84a10c430645a88bdbfaaa23ac9ff7.html
  3. After setup of communication arrangement, we will have the Sales Order Simulation API endpoint to be used inside Custom BADI.
  4.  Since, the Sales order simulation API to be called within the S/4 system itself, so custom communcation scenario to be setup to enable the call of inbound APIs inside Cloud Badi.  In custom communcation scenario, generic outbound service for REST type to be added. Communication arrangement to be created for this custom communication scenario to activate HTTP service with same outbound user as was used in step 1(setup of standard communcation scenrio).  Refer SAP Help document for additional details.  https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0f69f8fb28ac4bf48d2b57b9637e81fa/8d5d71252edd449982083cb5a3b8c8c4-182.html
  5. After completing the above steps, custom communication scenario, outbound service and Sales order simulation API is ready to be used inside implementaton of cloud BADI SD_APM_SET_APPROVAL_REASON."

Cloud BADI implementation

  1. Create Implementation of Cloud BADI 'SD_APM_SET_APPROVAL_REASON' via Custom Logic app (Key User Extensibility).
  2. Add required checks based on importing parameters - SALESDOCUMENT(Sales Document for Approvals)  and  SALESDOCUMENTITEM (Sales Document Item Table for Approvals). For instance : type of order, sales origanzation, sales document categoty.
  3. Configuration check of communication arrangment, service activation and API Call request-response. 
  • Check if outbound service exists in the communication scenario.

                      CHECK cl_ble_http_client=>is_service_available(
                             communication_scenario = 'YY1_COMM_SCENARIO'
                              outbound_service = 'YY1_COMM_OUTBOUND_REST'
                                    ) = abap_true.

  •         Creation of the HTTP client to access the outbound service
               DATA(lo_client) = cl_ble_http_client=>create(
               communication_scenario = 'YY1_COMM_SCENARIO'
              outbound_service = 'YY1_COMM_OUTBOUND_REST'
                 ).
  •          Creation of service request and set properties  
             *method used for service call
       lo_request = cl_ble_http_request=>create( ).                                                                            
       lo_request->set_header_parameter( EXPORTING name = 'x-csrf-token' value = lv_csrf ).
       lo_request->set_content_type( 'application/json' ).

             * Post method to execute Sales Order Simulation API. URL here is endpoint ('/api_salesordersimulation/srvd_a2x/sap/salesordersimulation/0001/SalesOrder' )
           lo_request->set_method( 'POST' )->set_resource_extension( lv_url ).

* Payload in JSON to be created for passing Sales order header data from SALESDOCUMENT and item data from SALESDOCUMENTITEM.

           Sample Payload :                     

{
  "SalesOrderType": "TA",
  "SalesOrganization": "XXXX",
  "DistributionChannel": "10",
  "OrganizationDivision": "10",
  "SoldToParty": "XXXXXXXXX",
  "_Item": [
    {
       "Product": "XXXX",
      "RequestedQuantity": 1,
      "RequestedQuantityISOUnit" : "PCE"
    },
        {    
      "Product": "XXXX",
      "RequestedQuantity": 2,
      "RequestedQuantityISOUnit" : "PCE"}
  ]  }
  • Call the request by passing Payload created and get the response. 
        lo_request->set_body( lv_payload ).
    * ***Send API request
    lo_response = lo_client->send( lo_request ).
    ***Getting response body from response received
    DATA(lv_body) = lo_response->get_body( ).                                                                                                  
  • From the API response, the value of 'TotalNetAmount' needs to be retrieved.
    The standard class method /ui2/cl_json=>deserialize should be used by passing the response body as input and extracting the value of 'TotalNetAmount' from the resulting structure.

Note: The custom wrapper class is created in ADT(Developer extensbility) to be called inside Cloud logic. This customer class used standard method /ui2/cl_json->deserialize_json to extract the attribute value of  'TotalNetAmount'.

/ui2/cl_json->deserialize_json(EXPORTING
im_payload = lv_body
IMPORTING
ex_total_val = DATA(lv_field_amt) ).

  • Compare the TotalNetAmount entered by the user during Sales Order creation (importing parameter: salesdocument-totalnetamount) with the Total Net Amount retrieved from the API response.  If there is a difference, set the changing parameter SALESDOCAPPROVALREASON  accordingly.

Conclusion : Additional specific complex conditions can be determined inside custom logic to check if Sales order workflow to be triggered or not. For instance, POST operation(simlulation mode) for released API's  can be called to validate conditions inside Custom Logic(Key user extensibility) in S/4 HANA Public Cloud System.

entry.title: Re: Your 2026 Roadmap to Getting Started with ABAP AI and ABAP-1
entry.content:
@freilinger Hi Sebastian,

the honest and short explanation is: I can only explain and repeat the official SAP strategy in a polite way although I understand that you and others ask for more.  What you might read between the lines is that we at least are working on covering more options (yes, for the cloud only) while at the same time the overall message and strategy is unchanged.

The extension to the short answer is: As product manager for ABAP AI I have the mandate to use the room that is given by the SAP strategy. But what you cannot see is that I capture the feedback I get from you and others in various channels and circle it back into the big SAP machine which finally comes up with new strategies and decisions.  Short term this leads only to "fine tuning" of what is possible within the strategy. But I do not give up also to influence future strategic decisions. My personal opinion is that we need to enable more customers to benefit from SAP ABAP AI to transform quicker to S/4HANA. Let's follow-up in a one-on-one to deep-dive more....

PS: @Stephan_Heinberg I understood your ask very well and I hope you did not see our conversation as waste of time! It was circled back.

entry.title: Re: Source of Information for Field in structures FPAYH-FPAYHX-FPAYP
entry.content: REF++ fields (FPAYHX-REF01-15 and FPAYP-REF01-05) are filled in Event 05 of Payment Media Workbench (transaction OBPM1 -> Event Modules). These fields are format-specific. Similarly ZREF++ fields are filled in Event 06 and are available for custom enhancements of standard formats (transaction OBPM3 -> Event Modules).

entry.title: Re: Passed Score 68% & Cut score 60% but Exam Marked as F...
entry.content:
• You’ve already done the right thing by raising a support ticket with SAP Training & Certification. They are the only authority who can correct or confirm the result.
  • Keep a copy of your exam report showing the 68% achieved score and 60% cut score — this is important evidence.
  • SAP will typically revalidate the exam session and update your certification status if it was indeed a system error.
